row.title: 3D Animator
row.description:
About Company

HB Studios is a driving force in the creation of sports video games and the studio behind PGA TOUR 2K Golf, presently working on Console and PC.


About the Role

We are looking for a 3D Animator to join our team of developers, creators, and fans who are passionate about the art and process involved in video game development. You will work with a team of professionals to build and maintain top quality animation that breathes life into one of the fastest growing sports game series in the industry.


Responsibilities

  • Create high-quality animation and collaborate across disciplines to meet project and aesthetic goals set out by production and leads
  • Build realistic human motion for gameplay and cinematics by editing and layering motion capture data
  • Animate props using rigging and constraint systems
  • Hand-key facial rigs and polish facial mocap data
  • Work within the game engine and pipeline to ensure all completed assets are in a build-ready format
  • Maintain a high level of quality within design and technical constraints while optimizing for performance
  • Collaborate with Software Engineers on the implementation, troubleshooting, and debugging of animation systems
  • Respond to direction with an open mind and an eagerness to refine character animation to ensure the highest quality and best fit for the game
  • Identify self-directed learning opportunities and provide input to improve animation production methods, tools, and techniques
  • Build positive working relationships and actively participate in team meetings


Requirements

  • Typically 2 to 4+ years of industry experience in 3D animation
  • Solid understanding of human body mechanics and locomotion
  • Proven technical proficiency with Maya or MotionBuilder
  • Experience working with motion capture data and modern game engines (e.g., Unity, Unreal, or proprietary engines)
  • Familiarity with source control software (Perforce or Git)
  • Technical knowledge of rigging and skinning is considered a plus
row.keywords: animation, motion, 3d, video game, character animation
row.skills: maya, motionbuilder, motion_capture, unity, unreal_engine, rigging, skinning, perforce, git

row.title: Senior VFX Artist
row.description:
About Company

Gearbox Studio Québec is part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. The studio is recognized for developing successful game franchises including Borderlands, Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, Brothers in Arms, Duke Nukem, Risk of Rain, and Homeworld. The team creates award-winning video games with rich universes expanded through transmedia storytelling, live experiences, and consumer products.


About the Role

The Senior VFX Artist works across all titles and interacts directly with the VFX Director, Art Directors, and Designers to create world-class special effects that push the technical boundaries of Unreal Engine 5, Xbox, and PlayStation. This role is open to VFX artists of all levels.


Responsibilities

  • Work with the visual effects team to create impressive real-time particle systems
  • Build elegant materials in Unreal Engine 5 for particle systems and world geometry
  • Create memory-efficient texture assets using programs like Adobe Photoshop, Substance Designer, Houdini, or Embergen
  • Produce basic 3D models, UV mapping, and simple animations when necessary
  • Meet style and quality targets established by the Art Director
  • Work closely with the Technical Art Director and Lead Artist to understand and establish production processes that consider the technical constraints of the game
  • Participate in meetings to discuss the implementation of visual effects with the goal of conveying gameplay concepts and meeting aesthetic objectives


Requirements

  • 6+ years of experience as a VFX Artist in the video game industry
  • Shipped at least 2 multiplatform titles
  • Experience with Unreal and Houdini
  • Experience with 3DS Max or Maya, Substance Designer, and Photoshop
  • Ability to collaborate within a multidisciplinary team
  • Curiosity and desire to improve methods
  • Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems
  • Demonstrable mastery of artistic concepts and common practices
  • Knowledge of the limitations of console games with fixed technical specifications
  • Good understanding of the general art pipeline in a game studio
  • Understanding of individual impact on performance in relation to the project's frame rate objectives
  • Enthusiastic, positive, and team-oriented attitude
row.keywords: vfx, animation, game development, video, 3d art
row.skills: unreal_engine, houdini, maya, substance_designer, photoshop, 3ds_max, particle_systems, uv_mapping, material_design

row.title: Sound Designer
row.description:
About Company

Gearbox Studio Québec is part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. The studio creates award-winning video games including Borderlands, Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, Brothers in Arms, Duke Nukem, Risk of Rain, and Homeworld.


About the Role

We are looking for a Mid-Level Sound Designer who excels at creating powerful, immersive, and tactile audio experiences. In this full-time role, you will be a driving force behind our game's combat feedback, weapon mechanics, and sonic environment. You will have a defining voice in our audio direction, experimenting with innovative systems to make our shooter gameplay feel incredibly visceral.


Responsibilities

  • Design, edit, and implement high-quality audio assets (SFX, ambient beds, UI, and cinematic audio) that align with the game's creative vision
  • Integrate audio directly into UE5 using Wwise, ensuring optimal performance and technical execution
  • Partner with Audio Programmers to design interactive systems, and coordinate with producers and leads to manage asset lists and deliverables
  • Own and track personal audio tasks, schedules, and technical documentation
  • Capture unique source material via studio and location field recording sessions to expand the proprietary library


Requirements

  • 3+ years of professional game audio experience, with at least one shipped title using Wwise and a commercial game engine
  • Strong hands-on experience with modern DAWs (Reaper, Pro Tools, etc.), audio plugins, and interactive audio implementation methodologies
  • Solid understanding of UE5 workflows
  • Diverse portfolio demonstrating high attention to detail, strong mixing capabilities, and deep understanding of sonic storytelling
  • Familiarity with source control software (e.g., Perforce)
  • Excellent communication skills and highly collaborative, growth-oriented mindset
  • Nice-to-have: Experience in linear post audio, basic scripting knowledge, or advanced UE5 Blueprint experience
row.keywords: sound design, audio, game audio, game development, creative
row.skills: pro_tools, reaper, wwise, ue5, sound_design, audio_implementation, sfx_design, mixing, perforce

row.title: Director of UI/UX
row.description:
About Company

Firaxis Games is a world-renowned game development studio founded in 1996 with an unwavering mission to build games that stand the test of time. Under the creative direction of industry legend Sid Meier, Firaxis has released dozens of hit games on multiple platforms and is recognized as an industry leader in game development.


About the Role

Firaxis Games is looking for a talented, motivated, and experienced Director to define the vision and execution for user experience (UX) and user interface (UI) design for a franchise. The Director of UX/UI will deliver world-class experiences for players while improving the outcomes, quality, and efficiency of the UX/UI team. You will operate as a Director, Lead, or Individual Contributor, focused on the Franchise or multiple projects, often switching contexts within the same day. You will direct work across all UI development regardless of discipline and define and drive the tooling and pipeline needs of the team.


Responsibilities

  • Lead a cross-discipline UI team of up to 10 UX designers
  • Work with Game Directors and Designers to translate goals and constraints into compelling user-facing experiences
  • Collaborate with other UX/UI Craft leads, ensuring strong cross-team relationships and communication
  • Present and pitch designs to the Creative Director, Game Directors, and Franchise Leadership
  • Produce UX design work such as competitive analysis, information design, content strategy, user stories, journey mapping, and accessibility standards
  • Work collaboratively across multiple teams within the Franchise to create consistency and resolve design discrepancies
  • Build flexible design systems that enhance the ability to develop design more efficiently and consistently


Requirements

  • 7+ years of experience working collaboratively in UX design, UI art, or UI engineering, actively developing UI features in video games
  • 7+ years of experience designing features and complex UX systems across multiple platforms (mobile, web, PC, consoles)
  • 7+ years of experience working in flexible environments and on multi-disciplinary teams
  • 5+ years of experience managing a team up to 10 people or equivalent training
  • 5+ years of experience presenting to other team members and stakeholders
  • Skilled with Unreal Engine's UMG system, Figma, Adobe Suite, and prototyping software
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating user-centered design solutions with strong visual direction and interaction design
  • Thorough understanding of UI/UX design, art, and engineering practices
  • Significant management and mentoring experience including disciplinary and career development practices
  • Strong understanding of game development processes and gameplay mechanics
  • Ability to advocate for the player and be their voice when needed
  • Strong alignment with Firaxis values and mission
  • Proven ability to manage, mentor, and influence cross-functional teams
  • Current US work authorization required
row.keywords: design, ux, ui, game development, leadership, interaction design
row.skills: figma, sketch, adobe_creative_suite, unreal_engine, prototyping, design_systems, interaction_design, ux_research, game_development, leadership

row.title: Junior Level Designer
row.description:
About Company

HB Studios is a driving force in the creation of sports video games and the studio behind PGA TOUR 2K Golf, presently working on Console and PC.


About the Role

As a Junior Level Designer, you will contribute to the creation of both authentic and fictional environments, ensuring that all course designs meet the highest standards of visual reverence and gameplay quality.


Responsibilities

  • Assist in recreating authentic golf courses for in-game use from real-world reference data
  • Build original courses from scratch, executing all phases of level creation from concept to implementation
  • Design and implement supplementary level designs on top of existing courses to support gameplay needs such as Challenges gameplay
  • Flesh out a course's visual needs in detail and work with artists to establish final appearance
  • Provide feedback on user-facing Course Designer tools based on hands-on experience using them
  • Use the provided toolset and operate on outlined processes for handling working files, implementing levels, and vetting check-ins
  • Assist in ensuring that tools and features work as intended and coordinate with QA team to log bugs and write reproduction steps
  • Contribute to the studio's growing philosophy of game design and how we make games


Requirements

  • Typically up to 2 years of industry experience in Game Design or relevant experience in level design and/or golf course design
  • Understanding of golf course design principles from a playability perspective
  • Familiarity with the public Course Designer tools in the PGA TOUR 2K series is a strong asset
  • Familiarity with the Unity game engine is a secondary plus
  • Ability to learn new software and technical processes
  • Clear written and verbal communication skills
  • Collaborative mindset with the ability to constructively receive, iterate on, and apply feedback from peers and mentors
row.keywords: design, game design, level design, unity, video games
row.skills: unity, level_design, golf_course_design, game_design, pga_tour_2k

row.title: Expert Animator
row.description:
About Company

2K is a diverse team of developers driven by a passion for creating amazing games that thrill players everywhere. The company pursues growth and innovation in an environment of safety and trust, built on the belief that varied voices strengthen their team and games.


About the Role

We are seeking an Expert Animator who can bridge the gap between compelling character performance and responsive gameplay. In this role, you will partner closely with animation, design, and engineering teams to create and implement high-quality keyframe animation for both human and non-human characters, bringing them to life through expressive movement and gameplay-driven performance.


Responsibilities

  • Create high-quality in-game animations that support responsive, polished gameplay
  • Implement animations directly in Unreal using the project's animation system and Blueprints
  • Set up animation blends, overlays, events, and related systems in Unreal
  • Iterate on animation and player feel through playtesting and hands-on tuning
  • Develop quick animation prototypes for new gameplay features, such as combat abilities, character actions, or cinematic sequences
  • Help define, maintain, and elevate the animation style and quality bar by creating clear examples, documenting standards, and sharing best practices with the team
  • Prototype and validate new approaches to character movement and gameplay animation
  • Partner with Technical Animation to explore and refine locomotion and overall character motion
  • Collaborate closely with fellow animators to elevate the overall quality of the team's work and foster knowledge sharing
  • Coordinate and maintain strong cross-disciplinary communication with Production, Design, Art, Audio, and VFX
  • Work together with character artists and riggers to establish core and secondary functionality of characters


Requirements

  • 6-8+ years of experience in game development, including at least one shipped AAA title in an animation role
  • Expert-level proficiency in gameplay animation using industry-standard 3D software, with strong experience in Maya preferred
  • Demonstrated ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced development environment, quickly prioritizing tasks and solving creative and technical challenges
  • Adaptable and responsive to creative feedback and changing project needs
  • Animation reel that demonstrates high-quality hand-key examples
  • Familiar with Unreal Engine, including animation systems, asset integration, and runtime considerations
  • Familiarity with Unreal Sequencer, camera language, and in-game cinematic workflows
  • Experience working with Perforce and managing assets in large-scale, multi-team environments
  • Excellent communication, collaboration, and organizational skills
  • Bonus: Experience creating cinematic sequences, character performances, and story-driven animation
  • Bonus: Experience with non-linear video editing, including pacing, shot composition, cuts, transitions, and visual storytelling techniques
row.keywords: animation, motion, video, gameplay, character animation, unreal engine
row.skills: maya, unreal_engine, animation_systems, blueprints, motion_graphics, character_animation, perforce, video_editing, cinematic

row.title: Game Designer (NPC)
row.description:
About Company

Gearbox Entertainment Company is a world-class team that entertains the world with award-winning and genre-defining video games. Known for developing successful franchises such as Borderlands, Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, and Brothers in Arms, as well as continued development of acquired properties including Duke Nukem, Risk of Rain, and Homeworld. The company is headquartered in Frisco, Texas and is part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc.


About the Role

We are seeking a game designer to join us in developing a first-person shooter. This role involves working in a team environment to design and implement new features and assets, working closely with many departments to iterate and improve the game experience related to non-playable characters (NPCs) specifically.


Responsibilities

  • Build and pitch new ideas, taking those ideas all the way through the production pipeline to a shippable result
  • Iterate existing game systems and create new ones based on project direction and feedback
  • Work closely with engineers, artists, and other designers to ensure features meet goals and ship on time
  • Develop new NPC systems, coordinate cinematics between NPC and mission needs, and build/organize data assets related to NPCs
  • Work closely with internal focus testing, community management, and other sources of player feedback to maintain and expand the online community surrounding the game


Requirements

  • Heavy investment in first-person shooters, both as a player and developer, with a deep understanding of the marketplace, history, and customer expectations in the community
  • Proven track record of modern systems design and/or scripting experience with portfolio including videos, screenshots, or documentation of systems or scripting
  • Shipped 1 or more AAA action titles in a game design role, with preference towards Unreal technology, first-person shooters, and online games
  • Excellent documentation, communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills
  • Ability to maintain focus and self-motivation towards current project goals
  • Strong problem-solving skills and keen attention to detail


Nice-to-Haves

  • 3 or more years of game design experience, including at least 1 competitive multiplayer title
  • Shipped games using Unreal Engine 4/5 technology
  • Level design, mission design, or cinematic experience
  • Experience working in small, rapid development environments
  • Prior leadership experience
row.keywords: game design, video games, unreal engine, npc design, first-person shooter
row.skills: unreal_engine, game_design, systems_design, npc_development, level_design, mission_design, scripting

row.title: Lead Animator
row.description:
About the Role

We are seeking a Lead Animator to lead by example through high-quality animation work while gaining leadership duties. You will help drive the project's visual style by providing regular feedback, mentoring team members, and contributing to the overall quality and success of the team's output. In this role, you'll collaborate closely with technical artists, game designers, producers, engineers, animators, and other cross-functional partners to bring creative ideas and gameplay experiences to life.


Responsibilities

  • Create high-quality in-game animations that support responsive, polished gameplay.
  • Implement animations directly in Unreal using the project's animation system and Blueprints.
  • Set up animation blends, overlays, events, and related systems in Unreal.
  • Iterate on animation and player feel through playtesting and hands-on tuning.
  • Develop quick animation prototypes for new gameplay features, such as combat abilities, character actions, or animatic sequences.
  • Help define, maintain, and elevate the animation style and quality bar by creating clear examples, documenting standards, and sharing best practices with the team.
  • Provide regular feedback in partnership with the Animation Director, mentoring animators, onboarding and developing team members, and serving as a trusted resource for creative guidance and support.
  • Interpret and translate direction from directors and creative leads into clear, actionable animation goals.
  • Help to organize and assign sub-team tasks based on individual strengths and project deadlines, assisting the Director in creating realistic timelines that balance delivery with team growth.
  • Partner with Technical Animation to explore and refine locomotion and overall character motion, ensuring the smoothest and most polished player experience possible.
  • Collaborate closely with Production on roadmap planning, task estimation, and risk assessment, while balancing competing priorities and adjusting workflows to accommodate evolving project requirements.
  • Escalate issues to the Animation Director when artistic decisions or direction are needed.


Requirements

  • 6-8+ years of experience in game development, including at least one shipped AAA title in an animation role.
  • 2-4+ years of experience in a leadership role and/or mentoring experience.
  • Familiar with Unreal Engine, including animation systems, asset integration, and runtime considerations, with advanced knowledge of Maya.
  • Nimble and able to prioritize and problem-solve quickly, while responding effectively to creative redirection.
  • Strong collaboration and communication skills within and across fields, with a proactive, solution-oriented mindset.
  • An animation reel that demonstrates high-quality hand-keyed animation.
  • Experience working with Perforce and managing assets in large-scale, multi-team environments.
  • Bonus: Experience leveraging Google Sheets/Excel to organize asset inventories, monitor production status, and facilitate coordination and visibility across departments.
  • Bonus: Experience creating cinematic sequences, character performances, and story-driven animation that enhances narrative and emotional impact.
row.keywords: animation, game development, motion, leadership, unreal engine, visual
row.skills: maya, unreal_engine, animation, blueprints, game_development, perforce, character_animation, leadership

row.title: Principal Technical Animator
row.description:
About the Role

The Principal Technical Animator (Gameplay) will help define the technical vision, architectural framework, and long-term strategy for character animation integration across the studio. In this highly influential role, you will champion innovation, driving the adoption of cutting-edge technologies to ensure games achieve world-class animation quality and responsiveness. You will serve as a technical authority on gameplay animation, solving complex R&D challenges while collaborating with design, animation and engineering to establish future-proof pipelines. While not a people manager, you will inspire, mentor, and elevate the technical capabilities of the entire animation department.


Responsibilities

  • Architect and oversee the high-level design of gameplay animation systems, ensuring scalability, performance, and seamless integration across complex real-time systems
  • Drive R&D initiatives to evaluate and implement cutting-edge animation technologies (e.g., advanced Motion Matching, machine learning/AI animation, next-gen physics)
  • Partner with Engineering and Tech Animation leadership to implement project-wide animation roadmaps and toolsets
  • Solve deep, complex runtime bugs and drive animation optimisation efforts to meet strict performance and memory budgets
  • Define and document technical standards, best practices, and pipeline workflows for the wider technical animation team
  • Collaborate closely with Design and Animation leads to heavily iterate on and perfect 3C mechanics (character, camera, controls), maximizing game feel
  • Actively mentor and upskill senior and mid-level technical animators through code/blueprint reviews and hands-on guidance


Requirements

  • Extensive proven experience architecting and integrating gameplay animation systems for AAA titles using Unreal Engine
  • Master-level expertise in Unreal Engine's animation suite (Anim Blueprints, Motion Matching, Control Rig, State Machines, and Sequencer)
  • Deep technical understanding of low-level animation concepts, including runtime optimisation, memory management, and threading for animation
  • Exceptional cross-discipline communication skills, with a proven track record of aligning Art, Design, and Engineering directors on complex technical visions
  • Expertise in feature breakdown and scoping, with the ability to translate abstract creative pillars into robust, production-ready plans
  • A profound understanding of gameplay mechanics, game feel, and the mathematical principles behind smooth character movement and physics
  • A portfolio or reel demonstrating a history of solving highly complex, innovative technical animation challenges in shipped titles
  • Strong proficiency in C++ for extending Unreal Engine's animation system, alongside Python for pipeline tool automation
  • Experience contributing to proprietary engine tools or custom modifications to Unreal's core animation code
  • Production experience with advanced DCC tools, including Maya API, MotionBuilder, or Houdini for runtime or pipeline integration
  • Background in traditional animation or motion capture data manipulation to better bridge the gap between art and code
row.keywords: animation, technical animation, motion, unreal engine, gameplay
row.skills: unreal_engine, c++, maya, houdini, motionbuilder, python, animation_blueprints, motion_matching, control_rig, feature_breakdown, scoping

row.title: Senior Gameplay Animator
row.description:
About Company

Cloud Chamber is creating BioShock games that move people: evocative, immersive, and meaningful experiences. Great games are born from great collaborations, supported by a culture that brings out the best in teams.


About the Role

You will be part of a remarkably talented team of animators tasked with breathing life into the world of BioShock. You will be responsible for creating exemplary gameplay animations and implementing and optimizing your work in Unreal Engine. You will work in close collaboration with distributed developers across disciplines to set standards that educate and inspire the team.


Responsibilities

  • Create gameplay, AI, and scripted animation that matches the visual style using keyframe and augmented mocap techniques
  • Work closely with programmers and designers to support gameplay features
  • Collaborate with Senior Animators on complex animation data sets for gameplay and AI systems
  • Participate in reviews with the gameplay team, giving and receiving constructive feedback
  • Communicate and collaborate with members of other disciplines as needed
  • Estimate time required to carry out tasks and meet deadlines
  • Provide knowledge and solutions for animation challenges
  • Work with programmers to establish and refine the animation pipeline
  • Develop high-quality gameplay animations that meet project standards and align with the animation director's vision
  • Integrate animations into the game with cross-disciplinary teams

  • Utilize motion capture data and hand-keyed facial animation techniques

  • Mentor, coach, and train junior animators


Requirements

  • Previous experience as a Senior Animator on an AAA title
  • Exemplary skills in keyframe and stylized 3D animation using Maya and/or Motion Builder
  • Experience working in Unreal Engine for animation implementation and debugging
  • Strong understanding of character performance, form, gesture, and silhouette
  • Excellent understanding of anatomy and caricature

row.title: Lead Sound Designer
row.description:
About Company

Gearbox Entertainment is home to a world-class team that entertains the world with award-winning and genre-defining video games. The company develops successful game franchises such as Borderlands, Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, and Brothers in Arms, as well as Duke Nukem, Risk of Rain, and Homeworld. Gearbox was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Frisco, Texas. The company is part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc.


About the Role

The Lead Sound Designer will guide the creative, technical, and organizational execution of sound design across one or more projects. This role reports to the Director of Sound Design and partners directly with Audio Leadership, Technical Sound Design, production, and cross-discipline teams to help define, execute, and maintain the project's sonic direction. The Lead Sound Designer acts as the primary sound design point of contact for the project, ensuring audio goals, scope, priorities, quality standards, and needs are communicated and delivered.


Responsibilities

  • Serve as the primary sound design point of contact for an assigned project
  • Partner with Audio Leadership to define, refine, and execute the project's sound design goals
  • Drive day-to-day project sound design execution while maintaining alignment with broader audio direction
  • Maintain a cohesive sonic identity across features and systems, ensuring sound design pillars, tone, and standards are upheld
  • Translate high-level audio direction into clear feature plans and create sonic style guide documentation
  • Work with production to identify and communicate audio scope, risks, dependencies, and priorities
  • Identify workflow and pipeline challenges and partner with appropriate teams to resolve them
  • Coordinate with outsourcing partners and provide feedback, reviews, shotlists, style guides, and quality control
  • Lead, mentor, and support sound designers across assigned projects, features, or content areas
  • Review sound design work and provide clear, constructive feedback that raises quality while supporting team growth
  • Help maintain team morale, trust, and creative momentum through strong communication and thoughtful leadership
  • Support onboarding, knowledge sharing, and alignment around project audio standards
  • Design, edit, process, and mix high quality sound effects
  • Collaborate with audio leadership on project sound design pillars, tone, mix direction, standards, and technical goals
  • Handle complex game feature sets of audio design such as weapons and character audio with complete autonomy
  • Create and maintain sound design documentation, including style guides, onesheets, shotlists, and palette references
  • Lead and contribute to sound jam palette building sessions and creative exploration sessions
  • Implement content into Wwise and UE5
  • Record original source and lead or support recording sessions, defining the project's source recording needs
  • Partner with Technical Sound Design on system behavior and implementation workflows
  • Locate, diagnose, and troubleshoot issues and audio bugs


Requirements

  • Previous experience leading sound design efforts
  • 7+ years of professional game audio experience with at least one shipped title
  • Adept ability at sound design, mixing, editing, and audio design techniques
  • Strong soft skills and ability to partner with audio leadership and cross-discipline teams
  • Excellent understanding of the end-to-end game development pipeline
  • Receptive to sound design feedback from peers and departmental leadership
  • Awareness of game industry trends, tools, techniques, and player expectations
  • Well versed at creating clear and thorough documentation such as style guides or system designs
  • Ability to communicate creative goals, risks, and priorities across disciplines
  • Bonus: Audio training and/or degree in audio production
  • Bonus: Experience with scripting or programming languages
  • Bonus: Post production mix experience
row.keywords: design, audio, sound design, game development, leadership
row.skills: sound_design, wwise, ue5, audio_mixing, audio_editing, game_audio, leadership, mentoring

row.title: Senior Mission Designer
row.description:
About the Role

Hangar 13 is looking for an experienced Mission Designer to join the team in Brighton and help form the core of a new design group. Missions are stories the player can experience that combine incredible level design, smart use of mechanics and interesting characters to surprise and delight.

Gearbox Entertainment Company is a world-class team that entertains the world with award-winning and genre-defining video games. Headquartered in Frisco, Texas, Gearbox Entertainment is part of 2K, a publishing label of Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc.

The Senior VFX Artist works across all titles and interacts directly with the VFX Director, Art Directors, and Designers to create world-class special effects that push the technical boundaries of Unreal Engine 5, Xbox, and PlayStation. This role is open to VFX artists of all levels.


Responsibilities

  • Work with the visual effects team to create impressive real-time particle systems
  • Build elegant materials in Unreal Engine 5 for particle systems and world geometry
  • Create memory-efficient texture assets using programs like Adobe Photoshop, Substance Designer, Houdini, or Embergen
  • Produce basic 3D models, UV mapping, and simple animations when necessary
  • Meet style and quality targets established by the Art Director
  • Work closely with the Technical Art Director and Lead Artist to understand and establish production processes that consider technical constraints of the game
  • Participate in meetings to discuss the implementation of visual effects to convey gameplay concepts and meet aesthetic objectives


Requirements

  • Shipped at least 2 multiplatform titles
  • Experience with Unreal and Houdini
  • Experience with 3DS Max or Maya, Substance Designer, and Photoshop

row.title: Senior Staff Software Engineer, Motion Planning
row.description:
About Company

Agility builds and deploys humanoid robots in warehouses, manufacturing facilities, and distribution centers to tackle physically demanding and repetitive tasks while enabling workers to focus on higher-value work.


About the Role

The autonomy team is seeking an experienced technical leader to set the direction for motion planning on humanoid robots. You will own the technical roadmap for collision-free motion planning across whole-body motion, manipulation, grasping, and loco-manipulation. You will partner closely with state estimation, navigation, controls, and behaviors teams to architect a planning stack that solves the hardest problems in real-world deployment: high degrees of freedom, dynamic constraints, environmental uncertainty, and reliability at scale.


Responsibilities

  • Own and drive the technical roadmap for collision-free motion planning across whole-body motion, manipulation, grasping, and loco-manipulation
  • Design, implement, test, and deploy motion planning and trajectory optimization algorithms for humanoid robots
  • Architect motion planning systems for modularity, observability, and clean integration with perception, state estimation, and control
  • Develop algorithms robust to environmental uncertainty and imperfect state estimation
  • Lead cross-team architectural decisions and collaborate with autonomy, controls, and behaviors teams to solve complex system-level problems


Requirements

  • Experience developing and deploying manipulation algorithms on high-DOF robots
  • Industry experience deploying mobile manipulation robots in real-world commercial applications
  • Proven expertise in sample-based motion planning, trajectory optimization, and model predictive control (MPC) with a track record of shipping these algorithms to production
  • Expertise developing planning and optimization algorithms subject to geometric and kinodynamic constraints, including contact, friction, and collision
  • Deep mathematical fundamentals in search and numerical optimization, including convex and non-convex problem formulation
  • Expertise in modern C++ (C++17/20) and Python for high-performance, multithreaded, real-time robotics applications
  • Track record architecting large-scale autonomy systems and defining clean interfaces across perception, estimation, planning, and control
  • Demonstrated technical leadership: setting direction for complex robotics systems, owning architecture, and mentoring senior engineers


Bonus Qualifications

  • Experience working with humanoid (or similar) robots
  • Experience with loco-manipulation: combined planning of locomotion and manipulation
  • Experience with whole-body model predictive control or whole-body trajectory optimization
  • Experience with task and motion planning (TAMP) under uncertainty
  • Experience leveraging machine learning or offline trajectory optimization to accelerate online planning
  • Experience with perception and scene understanding as it pertains to motion planning
  • Publications in top robotics venues (RSS, ICRA, IROS, etc.)
row.keywords: motion planning, robotics, trajectory optimization, manipulation, mpc

  • Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with General/Store Manager to escalate process or system gaps
  • Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management
  • Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor
  • Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities
  • Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes
  • Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests
  • Execute company-level operations-focused directives, projects and initiatives in partnership with the Operations & Visual Management team
  • Process shipment and ensure product flow, including visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence
  • Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business-driving strategies

  • Perform other tasks as needed to keep store clean, safe, inviting and operating efficiently

  • Self-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually and as a team
  • Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles


Schedule

Part-Time non-exempt associates are expected to work up to 30 hours per week based on business needs, with a shift minimum of 4 hours. All employees are expected to be available during the company's Blackout period from the week of American Thanksgiving through the end of the holiday season (first week of January).

row.description:

ALO is a mindful movement brand that combines retail, yoga, and wellness. The company's mission is to bring consciousness from the studio mat into everyday life, creating better living and stronger communities through authentic connection to yoga and wellness culture.


About the Role

The Sales Associate works to achieve store sales goals by setting the ALO experience and brand mission standard. This role creates an authentic, organic experience of excellence for every guest through genuine connection, product education, and community culture awareness. The Sales Associate is an expert in all facets of product knowledge, including features, benefits, fabric, usage, design, and care, and thrives in a team environment with a culture of feedback and excellence.

  • Ensure effective sales floor coverage and lead selling initiatives throughout the store
  • Confidently introduce all facets of the ALO business model (Retail, Yoga, Café) through personal testimony and knowledge
  • Educate guests and staff on product, community, and culture
  • Resolve client needs quickly and effectively to ensure customer satisfaction
  • Assist in processing shipments, visual merchandising placement, and daily replenishment
  • Execute all front and back-of-house procedures in accordance with company policies
  • Maintain store cleanliness, safety, and efficiency through constant movement and initiative


Requirements

  • 1+ years prior work experience in a client-centric, sales environment (preferred)
  • Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences
  • Alignment with and embodiment of ALO's Guiding Principles
  • Self-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually and as a team
  • Ability to lift, push, carry, or otherwise move up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to lift, bend, kneel, climb, crawl, twist, and safely climb up/down a ladder
  • Ability to stand and move for an entire shift with constant movement in all store areas
  • Open availability 7 days a week, 32-40 hours per week
  • Availability during company Blackout period from American Thanksgiving through the first week of January

row.title: Social Content Producer
row.description:
About Company

ALO is a mindful movement brand that believes studio-to-street consciousness transforms yogis' lives and communities through better living.


About the Role

The Social Content Producer will lead the planning, coordination, and execution of social-first content across a growing range of formats, including social content shoots, social-first campaigns, athlete content, and creator partnerships. This role is responsible for delivering high-quality content at scale while balancing creative execution with production operations.


Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end production of social-first content, from planning through on-set execution
  • Oversee all production logistics, including scheduling, locations, permits, crew, equipment, and talent
  • Ensure content is captured efficiently and aligns with platform best practices and performance goals
  • Lead content capture at high-profile brand events, adapting to fast-paced environments and evolving priorities
  • Support production of athlete content, including talent coordination and on-site execution
  • Manage creator content from outreach and briefing through final delivery, including timelines, approvals, and coordination with Influencer/Talent teams
  • Oversee multiple concurrent projects, ensuring alignment with content calendars and campaign timelines
  • Own projects with a high level of autonomy, bringing structure and organization to production workflows
  • Contribute to improving processes and identifying ways to increase efficiency as content volume grows
  • Partner cross-functionally with Creative, Social, Influencer, and Marketing teams to ensure alignment from concept through execution


Requirements

  • 3-5+ years of experience in social content production, digital media, or creative production
  • Strong understanding of social platforms and evolving content trends
  • Experience producing high-volume content and managing multiple projects simultaneously
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail and execution
  • Comfortable operating independently and driving projects forward in a fast-paced, evolving environment
  • Strong understanding of social content performance and audience engagement
  • Solutions-oriented with the ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities
  • Collaborative with a proactive and clear communication style
  • Experience working with creators, influencers, and talent preferred
  • Comfortable working on-location, including travel and non-standard hours as needed

About Company

About the Role

The Sales & Service Manager is a member of the store leadership team responsible for executing ALO's business and guest experience strategy. This role ensures exceptional guest experience in-store while driving sales performance and team excellence. The position requires constant movement throughout the sales floor and back of house.


Responsibilities

  • Investigate and root cause sales opportunities, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps
  • Oversee the Sales & Service team to execute exceptional guest experience through assessing guest connection and product education
  • Build client relationships daily and continue developing customer connections
  • Ensure effective sales floor coverage through workforce solutions and leading selling initiatives
  • Partner with the General Manager to build and execute the Sales & Service strategy for the store
  • Review, monitor, and manage budget, reporting, and business data including metrics and planning processes
  • Ensure the Sales, Service, and Community team embodies ALO's mission and guiding principles
  • Lead teams through accountability, coaching, talent development, and effective communication
  • Establish internal and external pipelines through succession planning and recruitment
  • Write effective schedules that prioritize business strategy in partnership with store management
  • Oversee execution of ALO Sales and Service Model deliverables
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to support organizational goals while maintaining brand culture


Requirements

  • 3-5 years of retail or related industry leadership experience
  • Working knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
  • Extraordinary interpersonal and communication skills, both verbal and written
  • Ability to handle multiple tasks in a changing environment with agility
  • Independent work ethic with strong time management and personal accountability
  • Ability to lift, push, carry, or move up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to lift, bend, kneel, climb, crawl, twist, and safely climb up/down a ladder
  • Ability to stand and move for an entire shift
  • Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles
  • Availability for opening, closing, and weekend shifts
  • Availability during the Blackout period from American Thanksgiving through the first week of January

row.title: Visual Lead
row.description:
About the Role

The Visual Lead is responsible for achieving store sales and maximizing profitability through visual merchandising in partnership with the Visual Manager. This is a full-time position requiring 32-40 hours per week, with variable shifts including peak days and weekends. All full-time employees must be available during the company's Blackout period from the week of American Thanksgiving through the end of the holiday season (first week of January).


Responsibilities

  • Manage execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving, processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra-day replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management
  • Maintain strategic product presentation and merchandising by ensuring product recovery, restock, destock, and visual merchandising changes in accordance with directives and partnership with the Visual Manager
  • Plan visual floor changes with the management team through payroll management and adapting zones and visual support to business needs
  • Execute and maintain visual direction from planning through execution while meeting company expectations, budgets, and timelines
  • Utilize reporting tools in partnership with management to identify business opportunities with product placement, outfitting, and stock levels
  • Partner with Operations and Visual Manager to manage reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities
  • Provide coaching, guidance, and direction to Associates; responsible for recruitment and development of talent
  • Evaluate operations associates using company performance tools and provide constant feedback to drive individual growth
  • Collaborate with Operations Lead and Sales & Service Lead to ensure cross-functional training and business execution
  • Open and close the store in accordance with visual standards


Requirements

  • 2+ years prior work experience in a client-centric, sales environment
  • Proven leadership capabilities demonstrating excellent decision making
  • Values feedback, receptive to receiving feedback, and eager to provide feedback
  • High energy, upbeat, and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work
  • Self-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually and as a team
  • Excellent interpersonal and written communication skills
  • Alignment with and embodiment of ALO's guiding principles
  • Open availability for variable shifts including peak days and weekends

ALO is a mindful movement lifestyle brand centered on yoga and wellness, bringing consciousness from practice on the mat into daily life through studio-to-street philosophy.


About the Role

The Visual Manager is a member of the store leadership team responsible for ALO's visual and product strategy. This role focuses on planning, product knowledge, analyzing reporting, and visual execution to drive efficient and effective visual merchandising. The position ensures the store is customer-ready at all times, requiring constant movement throughout the store including sales floor and back of house operations.


Responsibilities

  • Oversee and support execution of key operational and visual processes
  • Champion strong visual standards for the salesfloor by leveraging business performance data
  • Lead strategic product presentation and merchandising through product recovery, restock, destock, and visual changes
  • Plan visual floor changes through payroll management and adapt visual support to business needs
  • Own visual direction from planning through execution to meet company expectations, budgets, and timelines
  • Utilize reporting tools to identify business opportunities with product placement, outfitting, and stock levels
  • Represent the business in meetings and conference calls with General/Store Manager
  • Review, monitor, and partner with General Manager to manage budget, reporting, and business data including metrics and planning processes
  • Ensure the Visual team communicates ALO's mission and guiding principles to guests
  • Lead team through accountability, coaching, talent development, and effective communication
  • Establish internal and external pipeline through succession planning and recruitment strategy
  • Oversee execution of deliverables on sales floor, achieving KPIs while protecting operational efficiency
  • Collaborate and implement General/Store Manager's vision and work cross-functionally to support organizational goals


Requirements

  • 3-5 years of retail or related industry leadership experience
  • Working knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
  • Extraordinary interpersonal and communication skills, both verbal and written
  • Agile with ability to handle multiple tasks in a changing environment
  • Independent work ethic, time management skills, and personal accountability
  • Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles
  • Ability to handle constant movement in and around all store areas
  • Ability to lift, push, carry or move up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to lift, bend, kneel, climb, crawl, twist, and safely climb up/down a ladder
  • Ability to stand and move for an entire shift
  • Open availability and willingness to work variable shifts including peak days and weekends
  • Ability to work during company Blackout period from American Thanksgiving through the end of holiday season
row.keywords: retail, visual merchandising, leadership, brand, product strategy

row.formattedAddress: New York, New York, United States

row.title: Visual Manager
row.description:
About Company

ALO is a mindful movement brand dedicated to studio-to-street consciousness, transforming the practice of yoga and wellness into better living for yogis and their communities.


About the Role

The Visual Manager is a critical member of the store leadership team accountable for bringing ALO's visual and product strategy to life in the store. This individual is passionate about planning, product knowledge, analyzing reporting, and visual execution-all with a lens on driving efficient and effective visual merchandising. The role ensures the store is customer-ready at all times.


Responsibilities

  • Oversee and support execution of key operational and visual processes, championing strong visual standards for the salesfloor by leveraging business performance data
  • Lead strategic product presentation and merchandising by ensuring product recovery, restock, destock, and making visual merchandising changes in accordance with directives and in partnership with management
  • Plan visual floor changes through payroll management and adapt visual support to the needs of the business
  • Own visual direction from planning through execution to meet company expectations, budgets, and timelines
  • Utilize reporting tools in partnership with management to identify business opportunities with product placement, outfitting, and stock levels
  • Represent the business in meetings and on conference calls in partnership with the General Manager
  • Review, monitor, and partner with the General Manager to manage budget, reporting, and business data, including metrics and inform planning processes
  • Ensure the Visual team exudes ALO's mission and guiding principles
  • Lead the team through accountability, continuous coaching, talent development, and effective communication
  • Establish internal and external pipeline through succession planning and recruitment strategy
  • Write effective schedules that prioritize business strategy in partnership with the General Manager
  • Oversee execution of deliverables on the ALO Sales and Service Model, protecting operational efficiency
  • Collaborate and implement the General/Store Manager's vision and work cross-functionally with business partners to support organizational goals


Requirements

  • Prior experience in leadership within retail or related industry
  • Working knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel, and Outlook)
  • Extraordinary interpersonal and communication skills, both verbal and written
  • Agile with the ability to handle multiple tasks in a changing environment
  • Highly motivated by driving business in a fast-paced, innovative environment
  • Business owner mindset with an entrepreneurial spirit
  • Independent work ethic, time management skills, and personal accountability
  • Alignment with and embodiment of ALO's Guiding Principles
  • Ability to lift, push, carry, or otherwise move up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to lift, bend, kneel, climb, crawl, and/or twist, and safely climb up/down a ladder
  • Ability to stand and move for an entire shift
  • Proof of right to live and work in country from start of employment


Schedule

Full-time role with a 40-hour workweek consisting of 75% of hours managing the retail floor and coaching the team. All full-time employees are expected to be available for opening, closing, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change.

row.title: Analyst I, Directed Content
row.description:
About Company

AlphaSense is the world's most sophisticated platform for market intelligence and search built on proven AI, trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in New York City, AlphaSense has more than 2,000 employees across global offices and acquired Tegus in 2024 to advance its mission of empowering professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence.


About the Role

Reporting to the Operations Manager, Directed Content, you will be responsible for sourcing the highest-quality and most-relevant experts based on the scope of research projects defined by Project Managers. You will develop a deep understanding of the types of profiles most relevant to assigned industry territories and leverage research skills and an inquisitive mindset to find individuals who meet those profiles. You will be an energetic and charismatic communicator, meeting directly with prospective experts to evaluate their suitability for assigned projects and onboarding the best candidates to the network. This role requires proficient German language skills (C1/C2 levels).


Responsibilities

  • Conduct research to identify the best-fit experts for projects assigned by your Project Manager
  • Work with your Project Manager to confirm and refine the scope of projects as you conduct research and sourcing
  • Conduct email and LinkedIn outreach to prospective experts
  • Coordinate and conduct calls with prospective experts to validate their background and fit for assigned projects
  • Document experts' relevant experience in a clear and concise manner to facilitate evaluation by Client Contributors
  • Answer expert questions and handle objections to maximize the onboarding rate of high-quality experts
  • Negotiate expert rates to minimize costs while aligning payment to the value of experts' insights
  • Support the scheduling of calls between experts and Client Contributors for your projects
  • Monitor calls and troubleshoot any connectivity issues


Requirements

  • 2+ years of experience in research, recruiting, talent sourcing, market intelligence, or a related role requiring proactive outreach and candidate/expert evaluation
  • Inquisitive mindset with natural interest in learning and ability to quickly see patterns
  • Proven ability to manage fast turnaround projects, meet deadlines and targets
  • Demonstrated ability to handle and prioritize multiple tasks and deadlines simultaneously
  • Strong attention to detail with ability to discern and precisely document core elements of an expert's relevant skills and experience
  • Excellent communication skills, regularly engaging with industry experts to learn about their background
  • Goal-oriented motivation to identify and deliver the best-possible expert profiles quickly and efficiently
  • Proficient German language skills (C1/C2 levels)
row.keywords: research, recruiting, talent sourcing, market intelligence, content
row.skills: research, recruiting, talent_sourcing, market_intelligence, expert_evaluation, communication, german_language, linkedin_outreach, scheduling
row.formattedAddress: London, Greater London, England, United Kingdom

row.title: Directed Content Analyst - German Speaker
row.description:
About Company

AlphaSense is a sophisticated market intelligence platform trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. The company uses proven AI and search capabilities to deliver insights from public and private content including equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, and news. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in New York City, AlphaSense has more than 2,000 employees across the globe with offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland.


About the Role

The Directed Content Team is an integral part of Expert Insights, generating thousands of expert calls each quarter on high-value and strategic content targets. As a Directed Content Analyst, you will report to the Operations Manager and be responsible for sourcing the highest-quality and most-relevant experts based on the scope of research projects defined by Project Managers. You will develop a deep understanding of industry-specific profiles and leverage research skills and an inquisitive mindset to find individuals who meet those profiles. This role requires proficient German speakers at C1/C2 levels.


Responsibilities

  • Conduct research to identify the best-fit experts for projects assigned by your Project Manager
  • Work with your Project Manager to confirm and refine the scope of projects as you conduct research and sourcing
  • Conduct email and LinkedIn outreach to prospective experts
  • Coordinate and conduct calls with prospective experts to validate their background and fit for assigned projects
  • Document experts' relevant experience in a clear and concise manner to facilitate quick and accurate evaluation of expert profiles
  • Answer expert questions and handle objections to maximize the onboarding rate of high-quality and relevant experts
  • Negotiate expert rates to minimize costs while aligning payment to the value of experts' insight and background
  • Support the scheduling of calls between experts and Client Contributors for your projects
  • Monitor calls and troubleshoot any connectivity issues


Requirements

  • 2+ years of experience in research, recruiting, talent sourcing, market intelligence, or a related role requiring proactive outreach and candidate/expert evaluation
  • Proficient German speaker at C1/C2 level
  • Inquisitive: naturally interested in learning and quickly seeing patterns to identify the best possible experts
  • Energetic: proven ability to manage fast turnaround projects, meet deadlines and targets
  • Organized: demonstrated ability to handle and prioritize multiple tasks and deadlines simultaneously
  • Detail-oriented: strong attention to detail with the ability to discern and precisely document core elements of an expert's relevant skills or experience
  • Excellent communicator: regularly engage with industry experts to learn about their background and convey participation benefits
  • Goal-oriented: motivated by identifying and delivering the best expert profiles for each project scope quickly and efficiently
row.keywords: content, research, recruiting, talent sourcing, market intelligence

row.title: Operations Manager, Directed Content
row.description:
About Company

AlphaSense is a market intelligence platform trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. The company uses proven AI and search to deliver insights from public and private content including equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, and trade journals. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in New York City, AlphaSense has over 2,000 employees across offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland.


About the Role

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Generated Content, Operations Managers oversee Directed Content research pods. You will be accountable for the overall performance of your research pod, including managing and supporting a diverse team of bilingual Analysts in the delivery of high-quality, relevant content against volume and coverage goals.


Responsibilities

  • Manage a team of Directed Content Analysts aligned to sectors of coverage and structured in pods, including bilingual Mandarin speakers
  • Set and track performance KPIs for your team that balance output quality, relevance, and volume
  • Guide the professional development of Analysts under your supervision through coaching, goal setting, and actionable feedback
  • Ensure overall library quality and relevance by partnering with Project Managers and Sector Leads to adjust research focus and sourcing strategies
  • Coordinate with Project Managers to define and update coverage territories based on evolving coverage goals and team capacity
  • Hire and onboard Analysts
  • Partner with Directed Content leadership to define and update coverage goals, and align research pod capacity and focus to evolving goals
  • Partner with Directed Content Enablement to design and maintain process documentation and knowledge resources
  • Work with Director of Contributor Relations to ensure optimal Client Contributor coverage based on Directed Content coverage goals
  • Troubleshoot issues and devise process, systems, and workflow improvements for research pods
  • Review and approve expert rates over a fixed threshold, keeping average expert costs below $300 per call
  • Identify bottlenecks in Analyst workflows and address them to ensure high-quality and relevant content delivery


Requirements

  • Inspiring leader deeply invested in delivering high-quality, relevant insights to customers
  • Proven experience setting performance and professional development goals and motivating teams
  • Strong track record building culture and supporting infrastructure that enables collective achievement
  • Demonstrated ability to set relevant KPIs to measure success of both people and processes
  • Proven ability to build dashboards and analysis to spot patterns in productivity and output
  • Strong track record building processes that drive scale and efficiency
  • Proven ability to identify root causes of issues and implement sustainable solutions
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills to understand needs and drive alignment with internal and external stakeholders
  • Experience managing and developing bilingual teams preferred
row.keywords: operations, management, leadership, content, analytics
row.skills: team_management, performance_management, process_optimization, kpi_tracking, stakeholder_management, data_analysis

row.title: Project Manager, Directed Content
row.description:
About Company

AlphaSense is the world's most sophisticated market intelligence platform, trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers including a majority of the S&P 500. With AI-driven search and market intelligence, AlphaSense delivers insights from a universe of public and private content including equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, and trade journals. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland.


About the Role

Reporting to the Sector Lead, Directed Content, Project Managers serve as the process and project management backbone of the Directed Content engine. You will be responsible for defining the research scope and angles of Directed Content projects that guide the work of Analysts. You will understand what expert profiles are most relevant to client interest in your sectors of coverage, leveraging Director of Research insights, Client Contributor feedback, user engagement data, and the AlphaSense platform to guide comprehensive insights delivery. You will also ensure day-to-day productivity of Analysts on your team, coaching them on sourcing strategies and ensuring high-quality results.


About the Team

The Directed Content Team is an integral part of the Expert Insights group, generating thousands of calls each quarter on high-value and strategic content targets. The team is responsible for identifying, recruiting, and onboarding the best possible experts from around the world, ensuring interviews are of the highest-possible quality and relevance.


Responsibilities

  • Own your sector, staying ahead of key industry trends, companies, market developments, regulatory changes, and investment themes
  • Launch and manage research projects aligned to client demand, sector priorities, and evolving market dynamics
  • Define research angles, expert criteria, and vetting questions to ensure high-quality outcomes
  • Review and approve expert profiles, providing feedback to maintain a best-in-class expert network
  • Run regular team huddles, track project progress, and manage workload allocation across Analysts

row.title: Electrical Harness Design Engineer
row.description:
About Company

Anduril Industries is a defense technology company transforming U.S. and allied military capabilities with advanced technology. The company brings 21st-century innovation to the defense industry, designing systems powered by Lattice OS, an AI-powered operating system for real-time 3D command and control. Anduril delivers cutting-edge autonomy, AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military.


About the Role

Provide engineering expertise to design, fabricate, and integrate harness systems while representing the harness discipline on a project. Work in a fast-paced environment supporting new developments, active deployments, and customer-operated hardware, concurrently managing involvement in multiple projects at various stages.


Responsibilities

  • Investigate, plan, design, and develop harnesses to meet various technical requirements
  • Research, select, and procure appropriate connectors, back-shells, contacts, wire, shielding, and cable construction for products meeting environmental requirements
  • Develop and generate harness system block diagrams and wire diagrams for production release
  • Participate in design reviews and provide input to identify issues and drive design choices
  • Prepare harness assembly instructions and test procedures incorporating industry standards and best practices
  • Provide guidance to the electrical team in identifying electrical system requirements from stakeholders including engineering, product management, quality, and compliance
  • Own end-to-end electrical wiring and interconnect systems, including power distribution, grounding, shielding, and signal integrity
  • Perform electrical trade studies for wire gauge, shielding, connector selection, and topology
  • Analyze and mitigate electrical risks such as voltage drop, crosstalk, EMI susceptibility, and thermal effects


Requirements

  • Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or equivalent
  • 3+ years hands-on experience designing electrical harnessing for rugged commercial, industrial, aerospace, or military applications
  • Experience working directly with harness manufacturers to deliver high-quality, high-reliability products
  • Ensure design meets system-level electrical requirements for voltage, current, bandwidth, redundancy, EMI/EMC
  • Familiarity with common electrical bus interfaces (CAN, Ethernet, RS422, RS232, 1553)
  • Familiar with existing applicable harness standards and MIL-STD requirements
  • Able to apply knowledge of SWaP for power, network, and communications systems and designs
  • Experience reading wire block interconnect diagrams and wire diagrams
  • Exceptional organization and communication skills (written and oral)
  • Must be eligible to receive a US Security Clearance


Preferred Qualifications

  • Build 3D routed harness data files applying NX Cabling and Siemens Capital Harness
  • Experience with or proficient in 3D CAD wire harness routing, such as NX, Siemens Capital, CATIA, or CREO
  • Able to read and generate formboard drawings in 2D CAD for production
  • EMI/EMC principles and common electrical bus interfaces (CAN, Ethernet, RS-422, RS-232, MIL-STD-1553)

Responsibilities

  • Develop and review test setups, procedures, and data to transition products and systems from R&D to production

  • Work independently and collaborate with senior staff and leadership to solve interdisciplinary technical problems


Requirements

Required:

  • 5+ years of experience with complex opto-mechanical systems, performing optical system design, analysis, alignment and test
  • Experience building LIDAR, laser communications, or adaptive optics systems, with strong understanding of pointing, acquisition, and tracking
  • Expertise with optical design and analysis software, preferably Zemax or CodeV
  • Knowledge of STOP (Structural, Thermal, and Optical Performance) analysis
  • Knowledge of image quality metrics and data analysis methods

  • Eligible to obtain and maintain an active U.S. Secret security clearance

Preferred:

  • Optical component testing experience (spectral transmission, interferometry, etc.)
  • Optical system alignment and testing experience (EE, MTF, PSF, etc.)
  • Opto-electrical system modeling experience
  • Experience with radiometry and stray light analysis
  • Demonstrated team leadership experience and mentoring of junior staff
  • Knowledge of common materials used in optical systems and limitations of fabrication methods
