row.title: Senior Events Content Manager
row.description:
About Company

GoFundMe is the world's most powerful community for good, dedicated to helping people help each other. By uniting individuals and nonprofits in one place, GoFundMe makes it easy and safe for people to ask for help and support causes. Together, the community has raised more than $40 billion since 2010.


About the Role

We are seeking a highly organized, creative, and strategic content leader to own and drive content strategy across our community and events portfolio - brand events, field events, community engagements, and our flagship conference, Collaborative. You'll be the connective tissue between event strategy and the content that makes each experience resonate: from keynote narratives and session abstracts to speaker prep materials and post-event assets. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of storytelling, event programming, and project management - and who can translate complex, mission-driven themes into compelling, action-oriented content for diverse audiences.


Responsibilities

  • Define content themes, messaging frameworks, and programming strategies for all events - brand, field, and community
  • Lead end-to-end content strategy and execution for Collaborative, GoFundMe's flagship conference, ensuring alignment with business goals, brand voice, and audience needs
  • Shape and sequence agendas, keynotes, breakouts, and ancillary sessions in partnership with key stakeholders
  • Develop and submit session proposals - including topic briefs, speaker rationale, and learning objectives that ladder up to overall event strategy and programming goals
  • Integrate product positioning and key messaging seamlessly into event content
  • Identify, pitch, and secure keynote and featured speakers, managing outreach from prospecting through confirmation; build a long-term speaker pipeline
  • Leverage audience data, post-event feedback, and engagement metrics to continuously refine content strategy and session effectiveness
  • Collaborate with the Communications and Product Marketing teams on overarching themes, story development, and message consistency across channels
  • Write and edit content for speaking sessions, executive presentations, panel discussions, and event programming materials
  • Co-create and refine content with customers, external speakers, and internal leaders - translating complex ideas into clear, compelling narratives
  • Develop speaker preparation materials including briefing documents, rehearsal guides, and resource documentation
  • Produce event marketing content - email campaigns, landing pages, session descriptions, social copy - in partnership with broader marketing teams
  • Build and maintain content templates and toolkits that scale across a growing events calendar
  • Own and manage content schedules to ensure all deliverables and assets are completed on time
  • Maintain a structured, documented process for content reviews, approvals, and version control
  • Track key performance indicators (attendance, evaluations, NPS, session ratings) and share insights with stakeholders to inform future programming
  • Partner with Comms, Marketing, Product, Sales, and Customer Success to align event content with business goals
  • Build strong, trust-based relationships with customers, thought leaders, and external speakers to deliver impactful event experiences
  • Serve as a strategic advisor to internal leaders preparing for keynotes or executive presentations


Requirements

  • 5-7 years of experience in content strategy, event content management, or related fields (B2B marketing, brand storytelling, or corporate communications)
  • Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts
  • Demonstrated experience developing content for large-scale conferences, executive presentations, or multi-format event programs
  • Strong project management skills with proven success in managing multiple deadlines and stakeholders
  • Experience working within the nonprofit and/or social sector, with an understanding of the unique needs and perspectives of mission-driven organizations
  • Demonstrated ability to connect content strategies to sales and marketing goals
  • Skilled in creating and editing presentations (PowerPoint, Keynote, Google Slides)
  • Ability to collaborate effectively across departments and with external stakeholders
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and disciplined
  • Familiarity with event production and audience engagement strategies
  • Experience with content marketing platforms and project management tools (ClickUp)

row.title: Graphic Designer
row.description:
About Company

HelloConnect is a rapidly growing global Business Process Outsourcing company with a dynamic team that embraces innovation and leverages advanced technology to create exceptional experiences.


About the Role

You will be responsible for creating commercially relevant licensed product designs across apparel, home décor, and lifestyle categories. Working closely with licensing, product development, and account management teams, you will transform IP asset packages, style guides, and brand assets into market-ready designs while ensuring compliance with licensor requirements. You will also support product launches through digital and marketing creative assets, helping drive brand growth, product success, and customer engagement.


Responsibilities

Design & Development

  • Create visually appealing graphics and illustrations for licensed merchandise across apparel, home décor, and lifestyle product categories.
  • Work from IP asset packages, style guides, and brand bibles to develop designs that are both on-brand and commercially relevant.
  • Adapt and reformat licensed artwork for different product applications, ensuring compliance with licensor approval requirements.
  • Prepare print-ready artwork files that meet technical specifications for manufacturing and retail.


Campaign & Launch Support

  • Produce creative assets to support product launches across digital channels including paid media, on-site banners, email templates, and landing page graphics.
  • Assist in the delivery of marketing collateral for licensed brand campaigns, working to briefs from the Creative Lead and Brand team.
  • Manage your own project workflow: track incoming requests, meet deadlines, and communicate progress clearly.
  • Work closely with the Licensing, Account Management, and Product Development teams to ensure design output aligns with both commercial and IP requirements.
  • Conduct ongoing research into fashion and lifestyle trends to inform design concepts and keep collections feeling current.


Requirements

  • 2-3 years of professional graphic design and/or commercial illustration experience, ideally in-house within a licensing, merchandising, or consumer products environment.
  • Demonstrable familiarity with licensed IP - experience working from licensor style guides, brand bibles, or approval-based workflows.
  • A strong portfolio demonstrating design sensibility across print-based product applications - apparel graphics, surface patterns, or lifestyle product design preferred.
  • Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator and Photoshop essential; InDesign a plus).
  • Good understanding of print production requirements and file preparation for manufacturing.
  • Ability to manage multiple briefs and deadlines in a fast-paced, multi-property environment.
  • Strong communication skills and a collaborative, solutions-oriented attitude.


Nice to Have

  • Familiarity with licensing workflows and the licensor approval process.
  • Experience designing across more than one product category (e.g. apparel and home, or gift and accessories).
  • An interest in pop culture, entertainment, or lifestyle brands.

row.title: Senior Product Designer
row.description:
About Company

HackerRank helps companies like NVIDIA, Amazon, and Microsoft hire and upskill the next generation of developers based on skills, not pedigree. The platform is trusted by over 2,500 of the world's most innovative companies to build strong engineering teams.


About the Role

As a Senior Product Designer on the Pair team, you'll help design how people collaborate and communicate during a live technical interview. You'll identify problems worth solving and design innovative, high-quality solutions that resonate with users, partnering closely with product managers and engineers through interaction and visual design. This role sits at the heart of HackerRank's belief that the future is human plus AI: you'll design next-gen interviewing experiences where developers showcase their skills in an AI-first world, and you'll build those designs the same way, working alongside AI agents every day.


Responsibilities

  • Design and ship features that balance proven UI paradigms with new AI-powered interaction patterns
  • Translate design intent (layout, hierarchy, motion, interaction states, edge cases) into instructions an agent can build from, knowing when to show rather than tell
  • Iterate in tight loops: reviewing agent output critically, diagnosing why something feels off, and steering the next iteration instead of starting over
  • Build taste into the system by encoding visual and interaction standards into reusable prompts, components, and guidelines so quality compounds across the team
  • Pressure-test your ideas and refine through collaboration and research
  • Partner with engineering, product management, and design peers on both long-term strategy and short-term execution
  • Care about the full user journey: from the moment a candidate receives an invite through the interviewer's final read-out
  • Manage competing priorities and act decisively in the face of ambiguity
  • Contribute to design and product culture at HackerRank, including how the team uses agents to raise the bar


Requirements

  • 5+ years of hands-on industry experience as a product designer, with at least 3 years on fast-moving enterprise or consumer products
  • Demonstrated, hands-on fluency with agentic coding tools: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or similar
  • Portfolio demonstrating excellent visual, UI, and interaction design, ideally including interactive or in-code work, not just static screens
  • Comfort with ambiguity, working independently or across many stakeholders, and balancing established design standards with new paradigms
  • Proven ability to ship high-quality products in a self-directed manner
  • Experience designing live, real-time, or high-stakes collaborative experiences (preferred)
  • Ability to build front-end and work directly in code yourself, not just direct agents (preferred)
  • Experience adopting new tools fast and staying ahead of a monthly-changing agent landscape (preferred)
  • Experience creating design systems, components, or guidelines that other designers and engineers build against (preferred)

row.title: Senior Staff Service Designer
row.description:
About Company

Gusto is on a mission to grow the small business economy. The company handles payroll, health insurance, 401(k)s, and HR so small business owners can focus on their craft and customers. Gusto supports more than 500,000 small businesses nationwide with teams in Denver, San Francisco, and New York.


About the Role

A customer running payroll doesn't experience the product, CX, and AI as separate things - they experience one integrated service made up of many teams, systems, and handoffs working together. When those handoffs are well designed, the experience holds up. When they aren't, the customer absorbs the cost. As AI becomes a larger part of the customer experience, the design challenge changes. More of the experience now depends on how work moves between AI systems, product experiences, and human teams. This role focuses on making those interactions more coherent, resilient, and understandable for both customers and the teams operating the service. You'll join a team with strong executive support and an active body of work across onboarding, escalation design, journey measurement, and AI-enabled service delivery. This is the second IC hire on a foundational team growing from two to three during a major shift toward AI-enabled service experiences. What makes this role different is that you'll be building with AI, not just designing around it. The team is prototyping AI-assisted workflows that help product and operational teams think through servicing implications earlier in the development process.


Responsibilities

  • Shape decisions on complex customer journeys where coordination across people, systems, operations, and AI is critical
  • Build AI-assisted service design workflows and tools that help product and operational teams think through service implications before launch
  • Enable adjacent practitioners including product designers, product operations managers, and process improvement analysts through reusable tools, guidance, and training
  • Foster cross-journey coordination and learning to help teams work from shared patterns and operating assumptions
  • Create representations and frameworks that make complex, multi-team service systems legible and navigable for stakeholders


Requirements

  • 8+ years of service design experience, including meaningful work inside technology organizations alongside product, engineering, CX, and operational teams
  • Demonstrated ability to make complex service ecosystems understandable through maps, models, frameworks, or other working representations
  • Track record of influencing roadmap, operational, or investment decisions through strong judgment, clear framing, and articulation of tradeoffs
  • Experience designing or operating services where AI, automation, or algorithmic systems shaped work delivery and interacted with human teams and customer-facing experiences
  • Ability to operate credibly inside product cadences and delivery pressure
  • Strong communication skills and ability to influence without authority
  • Experience working with frontline and operational teams to inform strategy

row.skills: prototyping, service_design, customer_journey_mapping, ai_system_design, systems_thinking

row.title: Senior Brand Manager

Since founding in 2011, HelloFresh has grown to employ over 15,000 people from 70+ nationalities, fostering a diverse, fast-paced environment where innovation and smart, fast action are encouraged.


About the Role

As Senior Brand Manager for the Dutch market, you will take full ownership of all brand-related activities and drive Brand Consideration and Brand Equity, deeply embedding HelloFresh into Dutch culture. This is a strategic, commercial role operating within a fast-paced matrix organization, bridging local execution with global strategy. You will work closely with Growth Marketing teams to ensure high-impact brand equity fuels the performance funnel and drives scalable business growth.


Responsibilities

  • Drive local brand strategy for the Dutch market that aligns with global brand framework while staying hyper-relevant to local consumer insights and trends
  • Lead orchestration of the campaign calendar and end-to-end large-scale integrated marketing campaigns (360° ATL, Digital, Paid Social, and Activations) from brief to post-campaign review, including sponsorships and influencer collaborations
  • Design and execute tactical, high-performing D2C retail marketing campaigns that leverage performance mechanics and brand storytelling to drive acquisition, retention, and brand loyalty
  • Act as a master collaborator within the matrix organization, working closely with local marketing operations (Media, PR, Social, Product Marketing) and Global Brand teams
  • Manage, challenge, and inspire creative, media, and PR agency partners; set high standards for output, negotiate effectively, and ensure agencies deliver cutting-edge, cost-effective work
  • Turn consumer insights and campaign data into actionable initiatives; take tight control of allocated marketing budget, rigorously tracking KPIs and ROI to prove how brand creativity fuels business metrics
  • Monitor competitive activities and cultural shifts in the Netherlands to inspire the wider team with new developments and local brand partnership opportunities


Requirements

  • 7+ years of progressive marketing experience in fast-moving D2C and e-commerce environments
  • Master's or Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, or Communications
  • Proven experience navigating complex, fast-paced matrix organizations with high exposure to multiple internal and global stakeholders
  • Proven track record of delivering successful, high-impact 360° brand and retail campaigns with deep understanding of the Dutch media landscape
  • Strong commercial acumen with deep understanding of social media, customer journeys, conversion funnels, and retail mechanics specific to Direct-to-Consumer business models
  • Outstanding agency management skills with ability to manage complex contracts, brief creatively, and steer agency partners to deliver ahead of the curve
  • Data-driven approach to analyzing consumer behavior with ability to translate complex data into creative, bold marketing initiatives

HelloFresh is an e-commerce and culinary company providing meal kit delivery services.


About the Role

The Content Creation Manager is a critical, hands-on role responsible for the successful creation of all new training materials for onsite and online distribution across multiple disciplines. As a Mechanical Design Engineer, you will play a key role in the design and development of advanced robots. row.title: Senior Instructional Designer
row.description:
About Company

HelloFresh is the world's leading meal kit company active in 18+ countries across 3 continents. The HelloFresh Group includes HelloFresh, Green Chef, EveryPlate, Factor (ready-to-eat), Chefs Plate, and Youfoodz. The company is transforming how distribution center associates learn and perform through world-class training and modern learning experiences.


About the Role

We are building a dedicated Training Content Team within the Global Value Stream organization to transform how distribution center (DC) associates learn, grow, and perform across all four US brands. You will be responsible for the full lifecycle of operational training content: converting legacy materials into modern, multi-modal learning experiences, building net-new content for evolving processes, and creating visual training assets that make complex operations clear on the floor. Your assigned scope will be determined through the interview process, covering specific brands and operational domains based on your background and expertise.


Responsibilities

Content Design & Development

  • Own the full training content lifecycle for assigned brand(s) and operational domain(s): needs analysis, content design, module development, review coordination, version control, and update cadence
  • Convert existing Google Slides and Google Docs training materials into 360Learning-compatible eLearning modules, self-led courses, and trainer-led facilitation kits
  • Build net-new training content from approved SOPs and process documentation, translating technical operational processes into accessible, engaging learning experiences for Tier 1 DC associates
  • Develop content across multiple modalities: self-led async eLearning, trainer-led (ILT/VILT) facilitation kits, job aids, visual SOPs, equipment playbooks, and floor-accessible quick-reference materials
  • Build content for diverse, multilingual Tier 1 workforce with bilingual English/Spanish scripting and layout as a current production priority
  • Apply AI tools for content generation, video scripting, and media production

Operational Expertise & SME Collaboration

  • Develop working knowledge of assigned brand's operational environment: production processes, equipment, food safety requirements, and associate roles
  • Partner with subject matter experts-operations leads, sous chefs, CI managers, floor trainers, and quality teams-to ensure content accuracy, gather feedback, and keep training current
  • Conduct or support site visits to observe operational processes firsthand
  • Serve as a responsive content partner for process changes, new equipment, or updated SOPs requiring rapid training updates

Content Governance & Platform Collaboration

  • Maintain brand-specific content libraries, version histories, and document numbering standards aligned with FLT Main Document Record governance
  • Coordinate content review and approval workflows with designated operational stakeholders before training release
  • Collaborate with LMS team on content handoff: SCORM packaging, metadata standards, course upload readiness, and update requests
  • Contribute to migration of legacy Google-platform content to 360Learning

Potential Additional Responsibilities

  • Build and clear active content gap backlog for high-priority operational areas including WMS-based warehouse processes, kitchen equipment SOPs, and fulfillment assembly content
  • Own allergen-critical and organic-handling content requiring precision and FSQA coordination
  • Develop training for commercial kitchen and culinary production environments
  • Build content for warehouse, inventory, and WMS-driven processes
  • Serve as multimedia production contributor: video scripting and editing, screen capture, graphic design, and template development
  • Participate in research and adoption of AI-assisted content creation tools


Requirements

Required

  • 2+ years of experience in instructional design, eLearning development, or training content creation in corporate, operations, manufacturing, or food production environments
  • Demonstrated ability to translate complex operational or technical processes into clear, engaging, learner-centered content
  • Experience producing training content in at least one modern format: eLearning modules (Articulate, 360Learning, or equivalent), video-based training, visual SOPs, or job aids
  • Strong project management skills with ability to manage multiple concurrent projects, meet deadlines, and communicate proactively
  • Excellent written communication skills for diverse audiences from Tier 1 associates to senior operations managers
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, high-change environments where processes evolve rapidly
  • Ability to build effective relationships with operational subject matter experts who are not L&D professionals

Strongly Preferred

  • Direct experience in food manufacturing, meal kit production, ready-to-eat/commercial kitchen operations, warehouse/fulfillment, or DC distribution

row.title: Product Designer I

About Company

ICON builds BuildOS, a software platform used by architects, builders, and construction operators to manage construction technology and workflows.


About the Role

You will own the user experience for a core product area within BuildOS, leading end-to-end UX work that connects users to ICON's construction technology. You will conduct customer interviews, map complex workflows, build prototypes, test with real users, and write specs for engineering.   • Strong fluency with Figma or similar tool for wireframing, prototyping, and design systems
  • Experience designing for workflows with real constraints, not only consumer apps
  • Clear communication skills; ability to explain design choices to engineers, leaders, and customers

Helsing is a defence AI company. Their mission is to protect democracies by achieving technological leadership so that open societies can make sovereign decisions and control their ethical standards.


About the Role

As Senior Industrial Designer at Helsing, you will help define and shape the physical design language across their expanding portfolio of defence technology products. You will take ownership of projects from early-stage concept development through to production-ready execution, translating complex technical systems into refined, intuitive and visually distinctive physical products. Working closely with engineering, manufacturing and product teams, you will operate at the intersection of aesthetics, ergonomics and manufacturability, ensuring products are not only functional but coherent, considered and capable of operating in demanding real-world environments. Your contribution will extend beyond individual projects: you will actively shape Helsing's cross-domain design identity and help build a product portfolio that reflects the seriousness and ambition of their mission.


Responsibilities

  • Lead industrial design projects end-to-end, from high-level concept development through to production-ready execution, maintaining design integrity at every stage
  • Develop technically informed, usability-driven design solutions for advanced technology applications, ensuring products are functional, manufacturable and operationally effective
  • Translate complex technical systems into clear, intuitive and highly refined physical products that meet both engineering constraints and user needs
  • Define and evolve strong semantic design languages, detailed graphical applications and coherent product architectures that contribute to Helsing's cross-domain design identity
  • Plan and facilitate design sprints and workstreams, validating ideas through prototyping, mock-ups and field evaluation
  • Produce high-quality 3D models and visualisations using industry-standard tools to communicate, test and refine design concepts with cross-functional stakeholders
  • Collaborate with engineering, manufacturing and product teams throughout the development cycle to ensure designs are realised accurately and at scale
  • Balance competing constraints, aesthetics, ergonomics, manufacturability and operational performance, to deliver products that are both distinctive and fit for purpose
  • Contribute to the growth and coherence of Helsing's product portfolio, bringing rigour and design thinking to each new challenge


Requirements

  • Have designed and shipped physical products from concept to production, with a portfolio that demonstrates both creative range and engineering rigour
  • Bring strong 3D modelling and visualisation capability, with proficiency in tools such as Alias, SolidWorks, Maya, Fusion 360, Rhino or equivalent
  • Think in systems and hold the complexity of technical constraints, user needs and manufacturing realities simultaneously and make sound design decisions across all three
  • Demonstrate a refined sensitivity for semantics, detailing and graphical design application, with a clear point of view on what makes a product distinctive
  • Have worked effectively within engineering-driven development environments, collaborating across multidisciplinary teams to realise concepts at scale
  • Communicate design intent clearly and persuasively through models, visualisations and dialogue to technical and non-technical stakeholders


Nice to Have

  • Experience in defence, aerospace, robotics, mobility, medical or other advanced technology sectors where design decisions carry significant operational consequence
  • Familiarity with designing mission-critical or operational equipment, with an understanding of the constraints and responsibilities this entails
  • Knowledge of advanced manufacturing processes and materials, and how these shape design decisions at the concept stage

row.title: Senior Design Engineer
row.description:
About Company

Helsing develops artificial intelligence-enabled capabilities to protect and defend democracies. They are growing their US operations with a team of mission-driven professionals solving challenging defense and autonomous systems problems.


About the Role

As a Design Engineer, you will work on innovative UAV platforms, modifying and adapting existing mechanical and electrical designs to meet US customer requirements and specifications. You will design the mechanical and electrical architecture for various unmanned and autonomous systems, ensuring their design, implementation, and testing meets the needs of the end-user. You will work in close collaboration with systems engineers, embedded systems engineers, production teams, and customer-facing program managers. Your role will be crucial in guiding the team's choices and ensuring the consistency of technical decisions.


Responsibilities

  • Modify and adapt existing UAV platform mechanical and electrical designs and interfaces to meet US customer needs, specifications, and regulatory requirements
  • Own subsystems design and participate in the overall development of platform architecture, ensuring consistency in decision-making
  • Conceptualize and design UAV and supporting system hardware configurations through comprehensive trade studies and analytical assessments
  • Develop modular solutions using WOSA (Weapons Open Systems Architecture) standards to ensure interoperability and upgradability
  • Create electronic architectures and mechanical design concepts, specification design, and select appropriate components
  • Produce 3D and 2D documentation using SolidWorks or similar CAD/PLM tools, ensuring proper form, fit, and function of components by using analytical and simulation tools
  • Integrate COTS/GOTS equipment to meet high-level system objectives while managing size, weight, power, and cost constraints
  • Apply latest design and drawing standards, including ASME/ISO standards, GD&T, and strong consideration for manufacturing aspects
  • Design for manufacturing (DFM) and end-user field assembly, developing robust, scalable, and cost-effective components
  • Define and perform validation tests for technical maturation with general electronics and electrical troubleshooting, and mechanical testing
  • Create comprehensive design documentation in accordance with company guidelines and quality standards


Requirements

  • Several years of experience in mechanical and/or electrical design for UAV platforms, aerospace systems, or similar demanding applications
  • Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Mechatronics, or a related field
  • Outstanding hands-on experience with 3D/2D CAD and PLM tools (preferably SolidWorks/PDM or similar platforms)
  • Strong knowledge of modular design principles and experience with WOSA or similar open systems architecture standards
  • Proven experience integrating COTS/GOTS equipment into complex systems
  • Knowledge of electronic component integration, preferably using ECAD/MCAD interfaces for component realization
  • Ability to work independently, owning a project from concept to validation


Nice to Have

  • Professional working experience with US defense customers and familiarity with US military specifications and standards
  • Experience with unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) design, including airframe structures, propulsion integration, and payload mounting
  • Knowledge of export control regulations (ITAR, EAR) and experience working on US defense programs
  • Experience with schematic and PCB (Printed Circuit Board) design for embedded systems
  • Familiarity with EMC (Electromagnetic Compatibility) design and validation
  • Experience with actuators (brushed and brushless motors, servomotors, magnetic coupling) and sensors used in aerospace or mechatronics applications
  • Background in Battery Management Systems and power circuit design
  • Experience with environmental testing requirements (vibration, shock, temperature, humidity) for aerospace applications
  • Familiarity with FEA (Finite Element Analysis) for structural and thermal analysis
  • Understanding of airworthiness certification processes and safety assessment methodologies

row.description:
About the Company

IDEO is a design and innovation firm where teams solve diverse, complex challenges for clients and their organizations, working alongside thoughtful individuals who lead with curiosity, empathy, and humility.

Visual Communication Designers at IDEO make the biggest impact through visual storytelling. row.description:
About Company

IDEO is a design firm using design to create new products, services, experiences, and strategies. IDEO's internal Global Brand & Marketing team works to activate IDEO's science of design with creative people to make futures you can click, feel, taste, test, and believe in.


About the Role

The Senior Visual Communication Designer is directly responsible for the development and execution of the Global Brand & Marketing team's visual communications strategy. This is a contract-to-hire position at 40 hours weekly with the potential for full-time employment based on performance and business needs. You will work from the San Francisco studio on Tuesdays and Thursdays every week, with occasional in-person attendance for events and activities.


Responsibilities

row.title: Sr. Product Designer II - Design Systems
row.description:
About Company

Instacart is transforming the grocery industry by delivering an essential service that customers rely on to get their groceries and household goods, while also offering safe and flexible earnings opportunities to Instacart Personal Shoppers. Instacart has become a lifeline for millions of people.


About the Role

Instacart's Design Systems & Foundations team builds the shared components, tokens, and guidance that power customer, shopper, retailer, and internal tools at scale. We're looking for a design-first, technically fluent product designer to lead the evolution of our design system as a product-raising quality, usability, scalability, and adoption across multiple product surfaces. You'll partner closely with engineering and design technologists to ensure design-to-code fidelity, integrate guidance directly inside design and development workflows, and shape a durable source of truth that can power automation, guardrails, and AI-assisted workflows. You'll collaborate with an 8-person foundations team and cross-functional partners to unlock velocity and consistency for product teams serving millions of customers.


Responsibilities

  • Own and drive the design system roadmap-prioritizing by product needs, system health, and adoption friction-while aligning stakeholders on a clear, forward-looking vision.
  • Define and evolve system UX: components, patterns, interaction states, responsive behavior, motion, and accessibility defaults that scale across products and platforms.
  • Lead token strategy end to end (primitive and semantic), including theming, density, and extensibility, and partner with engineering to maintain consistent component APIs.
  • Establish strong governance and lifecycle management: contribution standards, quality bars, review processes, versioning, deprecations, and clear migration plans to reduce system drift.
  • Create implementation-aware specs with edge cases, constraints, usage guidance, and acceptance criteria; collaborate in component-based workflows (e.g., Figma libraries, Storybook) to ensure design-to-code fidelity.
  • Build design enablement in context-linting rules, templates, and plugin experiences-supported by a structured knowledge model (rules, constraints, examples) that powers automation and AI-assisted support.
  • Use AI tools intentionally to streamline audits, consistency checks, migrations, and spec generation; contribute to AI-assisted workflows that help teams adopt changes safely.
  • Measure adoption and impact, identify workflow friction, and execute enablement programs that reduce rework, raise consistency, and accelerate safe system evolution.


Requirements

  • 6+ years of experience in product design (or equivalent), including shipping and maintaining a design system adopted by multiple product teams.
  • Portfolio showcasing design systems mastery (components, patterns, tokens, governance) and cross-platform consistency; portfolio link required.
  • Technical fluency collaborating in component-based workflows (e.g., Figma component libraries and Storybook or equivalent) with a strong understanding of how implementation constraints shape UX.
  • Hands-on experience with token pipelines and tooling (e.g., CSS variables, semantic token architectures) and writing implementation-aware specs with acceptance criteria.
  • Proven accessibility competency applied to systems (e.g., WCAG 2.1 AA) with accessible defaults and patterns.
  • Demonstrated use of AI/automation to improve delivery speed and quality for design systems (e.g., audits, migration planning, consistency checks, spec generation).
  • Experience driving system adoption through metrics, deprecation/migration programs, and cross-functional enablement.
  • Bachelor's degree in Design, HCI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with AI-assisted coding and design environments (e.g., Cursor, Claude Code) and building Figma plugins or linting/guardrail tooling.
  • Track record implementing scalable theming, density, and semantic token strategies across multiple products or platforms.
  • Experience facilitating design sprints, co-creation workshops, or component governance reviews with cross-functional stakeholders.
  • Data fluency leveraging telemetry, adoption metrics, and experiments to inform system roadmap and deprecation strategies.
  • Familiarity with versioned monorepos or multi-package design system releases, including change management and migration planning.

row.title: Senior PCB Designer
row.description:
About Company

IonQ is the world's leading quantum platform and merchant supplier delivering integrated quantum solutions across computing, networking, sensing, and security. Vector Atomic, an IonQ company, is building quantum technology to transform navigation, timing, geophysical exploration, and telecommunications.


About the Role

As a Senior PCB Designer, you will collaborate with a talented team of experienced engineers and physicists to develop high-performance PCBAs and supporting electronics for innovative R&D initiatives and commercial products. This is a unique hands-on opportunity to bridge the gap between complex theoretical designs and physical hardware, ensuring that precision sensor systems are manufactured to the highest standards of quality and reliability.


Responsibilities

  • Apply established design rules, define PCB layer stack-ups, and determine via strategies including HDI to optimize performance
  • Lead and participate in engineering design reviews, updating layouts based on technical feedback and resolving Design Rule Check issues
  • Perform BOM scrubbing and review designs for manufacturability to ensure seamless production
  • Prepare comprehensive fabrication and assembly packages including BOMs, Gerber/ODB++ files, drill files, and manufacturing drawings
  • Work directly with PCB fabricators and assembly vendors to resolve build questions and address parts procurement challenges
  • Maintain organized design files, libraries, and documentation within the PLM system, supporting revisions and Engineering Change Orders
  • Partner with test engineers during board bring-up, troubleshooting, and validation phases in a laboratory setting


Requirements

  • Technical degree, relevant certification, or equivalent hands-on industry or military experience
  • 6+ years of professional PCB layout experience, including fabrication and manufacturing support
  • Proficiency with modern PCB design tools, Altium Designer preferred
  • Experience with high-density HDI layouts and multilayer boards
  • Strong understanding of grounding strategies, power routing, and general electronic systems
  • Deep knowledge of PCB fabrication processes and industry-standard manufacturing documentation
  • Proficiency with laboratory instrumentation and electronic test equipment; comfortable working in a laboratory environment
  • U.S. citizen, lawful U.S. permanent resident, or certain categories of refugees and asylees to comply with U.S. export control laws


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience designing boards with controlled impedance, including differential pair routing and tight length-matching requirements
  • Background in mixed-signal or low-noise analog circuit design environments
  • IPC certifications related to electronics or PCB design
  • Experience designing to or updating documentation for RoHS, CE, EMI/EMC, or UL compliance
  • Demonstrated curiosity and motivation to expand knowledge of electronics fundamentals and quantum technology applications

row.title: Staff Electrical Hardware Design Engineer
row.description:
About Company

IonQ is the world's leading quantum platform and merchant supplier delivering integrated quantum solutions across computing, networking, sensing, and security. Capella, an IonQ company, spans national security, advanced sensing, and next-generation compute to deliver secure, mission-ready intelligence. The company has achieved 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity, setting a world record in quantum computing performance.


About the Role

As a Staff Electrical Hardware Design Engineer, you will take ownership of full-cycle PCBA designs for spaceflight hardware, from early concept to flight readiness. This role is based onsite at the Louisville, CO office with up to 10% travel (domestic or international). You will work cross-functionally with RF, mechanical, software, FPGA/SoC, and operations teams to bring high-reliability, space-ready electronics to orbit, guide the Electrical Engineering team on special topics, and conduct cross-disciplinary reviews.


Responsibilities

  • Own the full electrical design lifecycle for complex spaceflight hardware including concept of operations, requirements engineering, schematic capture, layout, bring-up, test, and manufacturing handoff
  • Develop robust analog and digital designs for mission-critical systems under environmental and performance constraints
  • Organize collaborative cross-disciplinary design reviews and shape solutions with experienced engineers
  • Lead board-level bring-up and debugging, integrating firmware, software, and FPGA with embedded systems
  • Define test plans and verification strategies that meet flight readiness requirements
  • Play a key role in scaling up manufacturing facility, bringing hardware from prototype to low-volume production
  • Collaborate across teams to ensure seamless integration of mechanical, RF, software, FPGA/SoC, and systems components
  • Mentor early-career engineers and contribute to internal process improvements
  • Engage with external vendors and contract manufacturers, ensuring delivery quality and resolving DFM/DFT issues
  • Drive failure analysis and root-cause investigations across flight or test anomalies


Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in electrical engineering or related field
  • 8+ years of experience designing and debugging PCBAs
  • Proficiency in Altium Designer or similar ECAD tools
  • Hands-on experience with lab instruments such as oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and power analyzers
  • Solid understanding of digital buses including I2C, SPI, UART, RS422, CAN, USB, PCIe, and Ethernet
  • Familiarity with board bring-up, test planning, and design verification workflows
  • Experience interacting with contract manufacturers and supporting DFM reviews
  • Strong documentation habits and organizational skills
  • Master's degree in electrical engineering or related field (preferred)
  • Experience designing for harsh environments such as thermal, vacuum, and vibration
  • Familiarity with high-speed design or impedance-controlled layouts
  • Basic scripting or automation in Python or C for hardware test workflows
  • Exposure to radiation effects and mitigation in electronic design
  • Familiarity with EMI/EMC design practices and validation
  • Familiarity with satellite electrical systems engineering including propulsion, Electrical Power System (EPS), Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS), Attitude Determination and Control System (ADCS), and space robotics


Additional Information

To conform to U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), employees must be a U.S. citizen, lawful U.S. permanent resident, or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum, or be eligible to obtain required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State and/or U.S. Department of Commerce.

About Company

Marqeta is on a mission to change the way money moves. We're one of the earliest enablers of embedded finance. Our card issuing platform provides unprecedented flexibility and control for companies to issue cards, authorize transactions, and manage payment operations in real time. Marqeta is powering the most well known brands in the new economy (Block, Cash App, Affirm, Instacart, Doordash, Uber, Walmart, etc).


About the Role

As Marqeta's Senior Technical Writer, you will shape how developers build on our platform by creating and scaling documentation for APIs, SDKs, and platform experiences. Our documentation is a core product surface and often the first interface that developers encounter, so it must clearly reflect the system's actual behavior. This role goes beyond writing. You will improve how documentation is structured, generated, and maintained at scale using tooling, automation, and AI, while driving clarity, usability, and self-serve integration. You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Design as part of a collaborative team that treats documentation as a critical part of the developer experience. Responsibilities

  • Use docs-as-code and AI-driven tooling to improve accuracy and reduce drift
  • Apply AI and automation to scale content creation and updates
  • Identify gaps through customer feedback and drive continuous improvement
  • Align cross-functionally on documentation priorities and standards


Requirements

row.title: Design Lead, Consumer
row.description:
About Company

Maven is the world's largest virtual clinic for women and families on a mission to make healthcare work for all of us. Maven's award-winning digital programs provide clinical, emotional, and financial support spanning fertility & family building, maternity & newborn care, parenting & pediatrics, and menopause & midlife. More than 2,000 employers and health plans trust Maven's end-to-end platform to improve clinical outcomes, reduce healthcare costs, and provide equity in benefits programs.


About the Role

We're looking for a founding Product Design Lead to help build and scale Maven's direct-to-consumer healthcare experiences. This role sits at the intersection of consumer growth, healthcare access, product craft, AI-native ways of working, and trust-sensitive design. It is a hands-on player-coach role with formal line management responsibility. You'll lead a small team of senior Product Design/UXR practitioners while also owning high-priority product design work yourself. You'll report to the General Manager, Consumer and partner closely with Product, Engineering, Growth, Data, Clinical, Legal, Operations, and Support teams.

You will help Maven compete in a fast-moving and highly competitive consumer healthcare market while staying true to what makes Maven different: connected, clinically responsible, whole-person care for women. You will be designing for users who are actively comparing options, making decisions quickly, weighing affordability, navigating uncertainty, and deciding whether they trust Maven enough to start and continue care. The work will span both 0→1 product development and continuous optimization of live consumer journeys.


Responsibilities

  • Lead a small team of senior Product Design and UXR practitioners while owning high-priority product design work yourself
  • Define new consumer healthcare journeys, shape concepts, prototype, and bring new offerings to market
  • Improve existing funnels, onboarding, eligibility, pricing, payment, prescription, activation, retention, and cross-sell experiences through research, experimentation, data, and design craft
  • Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Growth, Data, Clinical, Legal, Operations, and Support teams
  • Drive both 0→1 product development and continuous optimization of live consumer journeys


Requirements

  • 7+ years of product design experience, including complex consumer product journeys and 0→1 product growth
  • Experience formally managing or leading design teams
  • Strong hands-on product design craft across UX, prototyping, visual design, mobile apps, and responsive web
  • Portfolio showing D2C product design leadership, systems thinking, data-driven decision-making, high-quality execution, and measurable product impact
  • Experience designing products where trust, comprehension, eligibility, pricing, access, personal data, or complex decision-making play a central role
  • Experience with growth, onboarding, conversion, activation, retention, cross-sell, monetization, or lifecycle journeys, informed by research, data, and experimentation
  • Strong AI-native workflow and informed opinions on how AI can improve design quality, team capacity, and business value


Preferred Experience

  • Healthcare, women's health, telehealth, digital health, insurance, fintech, marketplaces, consumer subscriptions, or another regulated or trust-sensitive domain
  • Designing products involving insurance, benefits, prescriptions, clinical eligibility, payments, care navigation, or personal health information
  • Designing in a competitive direct-to-consumer category where acquisition, conversion, trust, differentiation, and retention all matter

About the Role

Design is central to how Maven earns member trust, differentiates in market, and delivers on its clinical mission at scale. This is an AI-native role where you will use AI to move faster, think bigger, and raise the bar on what a small, high-performing team can produce. You'll modernize how the design organization works from research to production to cross-functional collaboration, and help the rest of the company understand what's possible when design operates at its best. This role reports to the Chief Product Officer and sits at the intersection of business strategy, product craft, and brand. You'll lead a team of roughly 15 designers, translate company goals into design priorities, and bring a point of view that influences decisions at the executive level.


Responsibilities

  • Create the conditions to ship faster without sacrificing craft
  • Embed user research as a core input to every decision

Maven is the world's largest virtual clinic for women and families on a mission to make healthcare work for all of us. About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Staff Product Designer to take full ownership of our member-facing experience and help shape the future of AI-native design at Maven. You'll bring deep AI fluency and use it to move faster, explore more broadly, and build more intelligently, while also mentoring others on how to work that way. row.title: Freelance Project Manager and Social Content Lead | Monks
row.description: Please note that we will never request payment or bank account information at any stage of the recruitment process. As we continue to grow our teams, we urge you to be cautious of fraudulent job postings or recruitment activities that misuse our company name and information. Please protect your personal information during any recruitment process. While Monks may contact potential candidates via LinkedIn, all applications must be submitted through our official website (monks.com/careers).About the Role We are looking for a freelance, hybrid Freelance Content Creator, Community Manager, and Project Manager to join our team and lead a project for Always On social media accounts (Instagram, TikTok, X, and Threads). This person will be responsible for the end-to-end project management, from leading timelines and client relations to creating content calendars, writing copy, and coordinating with the internal creative teams. The role will report to and be under the supervision of the Content Lead, who will guide strategic implementation and format updates. The profile will have ad-hoc support from a content assistant whenever the workload or client requests require it. Constant communication with Madrid's project management team. Schedule: A dedication of 4 working hours per day is required. We propose that this schedule be carried out continuously from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM, Monday to Friday, to ensure perfect alignment with both the client and the internal Team. Responsibilities: Project Management and Client Relations Project Leadership: Act as the main Point of Contact (POC) for the client, ensuring a smooth communication flow and strict compliance with all delivery deadlines. Status Calls: Prepare and lead the weekly status call with the client (Fridays at 12:00 PM) to present internal proposals and calendars for final approval. Request Management: Handle emails and activate client requests with the content team (such as sourcing global toolkits or pushing editorial playlists). Campaigns/extra requirements: Lead and coordinate the execution of any specific campaigns/activations located in the local market. This will be assigned considering the workload in the specific moment. Content Creation and Planning (Content Creator) Weekly and Monthly Calendars: Develop content calendars delivered weekly to the client, while maintaining an internal monthly forecast and structure to ensure a balanced distribution of content throughout the month. Copywriting: Write the copy for all posts (Brand Love, artist-focused copy, New Music Fridays, etc.) tailored to the required platforms: Instagram, TikTok, X, and Threads (content on Threads will strictly replicate X). Languages: Polish and English. Reactivity: Quickly integrate copy revisions, last-minute changes, and new priorities that frequently arise from the client side. RTM: Proposal of at least 2 reactive contents per month related to trends or conversations in Poland. Community Management and Quality Assurance (QA) Social Monitoring: Conduct daily reviews of social channels to detect unexpected conversations or brand mentions, as well as support with reactive publishing when necessary. QA (Pre & Post Publishing): Verify that scheduled content exactly matches client-approved material (typos, artist names, and @ handles), publish successfully, confirm with the team that everything is live, and monitor comments/DMs for any immediate issues. CM: Propose and execute social media comments to engage with artists and fans following the brand guidelines. Internal Team Coordination (Design and Video) Asset Briefing: Responsible for formally briefing (via email) the internal design and video editing teams to prepare all necessary creative assets. Quality Control (QC): Lead the quality control of deliverables from the creative team, ensuring assets meet the requirements before presenting them in Friday's calendar. About You The essentials: Native Polish and fluent professional English is a must. Deep knowledge of Gen Z culture and social communication Deep knowledge of the Polish music scene, plus experience in the music industry Technical & Content Skills Content Management: Prepare social media content calendars and take full ownership of the brand's tone of voice. Platform Expertise: Deep understanding of social media "always-on" retainers and campaign management. Culture Specialist: Must be highly plugged into Polish culture and on top of local news/trends. Production Workflow: Ability to coordinate with creative teams and designers to deliver assets. Project Management (PM) Skills Client Management: Act as the primary Polish-speaking point of contact for the client; must be proactive and responsive. Workflow Oversight: Manage daily priorities, task delivery, and the internal workflow between the agency and the client. Execution: Handling "hands-on" delivery of social content and posting. At Monks, we believe in fostering an environment where a diversity of perspectives can thrive. We proactively work to design hiring processes that promote equity and inclusion while mitigating bias. We celebrate diversity and are committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage qualified applicants, from all backgrounds, who are excited to contribute to our mission. #LI-JT1About Monks Monks is the global, digital-first, data-driven, unitary operating brand of S4 Capital plc. With a legacy of innovation and specialized expertise, Monks combines an extraordinary range of global Marketing and Technology Services to redefine how brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its flagship AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine, collapsing timelines and connecting brands to culture in real time. By deploying bespoke intelligent agents across disciplines and delivering culturally relevant, high-impact creative and digital solutions, Monks solves key critical business challenges across the entire brand enterprise to help brands sustain long-term impact. Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave™: Global Marketing Services, ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies (2022-25) and remains the only partner featured in AdExchanger’s Programmatic Power Players list every year (2020-24). Named Adweek’s first AI Agency of the Year (2023) and The One Show’s inaugural AI Pioneer Organization, Monks was also awarded Business Intelligence Group’s 2025 Excellence in Artificial Intelligence Award in both the Organizational and AI Product categories. As a trusted partner to cutting-edge innovators in tech, Monks earned titles such as Optimizely Experimentation Partner of the Year (2025), runner-up for the Adobe Firefly Partner Award (2024), and Workato’s AI Visionary Customer Impact Award (2024). Additionally, Monks achieved a record-breaking number of FWAs and continues to hold the most of any partner. We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to building a respectful and empowering work environment for all people to freely express themselves amongst colleagues who embrace diversity in all respects. Including fresh voices and unique points of view in all aspects of our business not only creates an environment where we can all grow and thrive but also increases our potential to produce work that better represents—and resonates with—the world around us.

You'll be a senior creative force on high-profile brand marketing campaigns spanning social content, hero video, motion graphics, and typographic animation. Operating within a dedicated client team, you'll own the animation process end-to-end - from concept and pre-production through final delivery - collaborating with creative directors, producers, illustrators, and animators across a global team. At this level, nobody oversees your craft - you set the bar. You're expected to drive quality and efficiency, mentor junior and semi-senior animators, push new techniques and tools, and serve as an internal ambassador for generative AI in the animation workflow.


Responsibilities

  • Own the full animation lifecycle - pre-production assessment, creative scoping, execution, and final delivery - across social assets, complex hero videos, and multi-platform campaign toolkits.

  • Hands-on experience with generative AI tools - Runway, MidJourney, Adobe Firefly, Whisk, or similar - with a genuine drive to stay current as the toolset evolves.
  • Strong visual design sensibility across typography, colour, layout, and composition in both static and motion contexts.

row.description:
About Company

Monks is the global, digital-first, data-driven, unitary operating brand of S4 Capital plc. It combines marketing and technology services to redefine how brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its flagship AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine. Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave: Global Marketing Services, ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies (2022-25), and was named Adweek's first AI Agency of the Year (2023).


About the Role

As a Senior Production Designer, you will contribute to the technical execution and scaling of innovative digital ads and assets for one of the world's largest packaged goods companies. This role demands expertise in balancing efficiency, speed, precision, and scale to produce high-quality visual content that resonates across multiple platforms. You will be passionate about seeking new solutions to production challenges, contributing to an AI-forward team, and creating impactful work.


Responsibilities

  • Run the technical execution, versioning, and rollout of design projects for products, features, and campaigns.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including marketing and production, to ensure designs meet client expectations and adhere to brand guidelines.
  • Execute master creatives across various formats while providing innovative ideas and best practices to improve the design pipeline.
  • Translate complex creative briefs into actionable and thorough production workflows.
  • Ensure pixel-perfect execution of designs, maintaining quality and attention to detail at all times.
  • Specialise in crafting and adapting interactive assets for social media and other digital channels.
  • Create designs and precise technical setups for typical POS print media.
  • Build and maintain robust templates, style guides, and asset libraries to ensure consistency and efficiency.
  • Mentor and support junior designers, providing guidance and feedback to foster skill development within the team.
  • Manage multiple projects simultaneously, prioritising tasks to meet deadlines and deliver high-quality work.


Requirements

  • 5+ years of experience as a Production Designer or similar role, ideally in a digital or advertising environment.
  • Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, particularly Figma and InDesign, Illustrator, and Photoshop.
  • Strong portfolio showcasing expertise in digital production, asset adaptation, and advertising materials.
  • Proven experience with the execution stages of the design process, from master asset handover to final delivery.
  • Deep understanding of design fundamentals, including typography, grid systems, layout, and pre-press standards.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills for effectively collaborating with clients and team members.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and adapt to changing project needs and priorities.
  • Experience with creating print-ready files for POS in Indesign/Illustrator.
  • Familiarity with current social media specs and best practices for visual content.
  • Interest and borderline obsession with evolving AI and automation technology and functionality.
  • Ability to guide and encourage younger design team members, oversee their work and provide constructive feedback.
  • Bonus: Some basic knowledge of front-end development (HTML/CSS).
  • Bonus: Knowledge of and experience in UX/UI.
  • Bonus: Experience with automated versioning tools or scripting to scale production workflows.
  • Bonus: Experience with generative or agentic AI tools.
row.keywords: design, production, advertising, digital, brand, asset management, ai
row.skills: figma, adobe_creative_suite, photoshop, illustrator, indesign, typography, print_production, social_media, html, css, design_systems, asset_adaptation

row.title: Video Producer & Post-Production Specialist
row.description:
About Company

Monks is a global, digital-first, data-driven marketing and technology services company that combines marketing and technology expertise to help brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine and delivers culturally relevant, high-impact creative and digital solutions.


About the Role

As a Video Producer & Post-Production Specialist, you will be responsible for the production and post-production of audiovisual content for digital, social, corporate, and advertising campaigns. You will oversee projects from concept execution through final delivery, ensuring creative excellence, technical quality, and alignment with client objectives.


Responsibilities

  • Execute video shoots, interviews, branded content, and campaign assets
  • Edit and adapt content for multiple platforms and formats
  • Create motion graphics, titles, and basic animations
  • Perform color correction, sound editing, and final mastering
  • Manage post-production timelines and delivery schedules
  • Collaborate with creative, strategy, account, and production teams
  • Ensure all deliverables meet brand guidelines and quality standards
  • Optimize workflows to deliver high-volume content efficiently


Requirements

  • Strong video editing and storytelling capabilities
  • Expertise in Adobe Premiere Pro, Da Vinci Resolve, After Effects, and related post-production tools
  • Strong understanding of digital trends, platform-native content, and audiovisual language, with a proactive approach to staying current on emerging formats and audience behaviors
  • Understanding of social media, digital content, and advertising formats
  • Excellent organization and project management skills
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced agency environment
  • Strong attention to detail and problem-solving abilities

row.title: Federal Brief Writer Attorney
row.description:
About Company

Morgan & Morgan is a leading personal injury law firm dedicated to protecting consumer rights. The firm serves millions of Americans against insurance companies, large corporations, and defective goods, with over 6,000 employees united by the mission: For the People.


About the Role

Morgan & Morgan is looking for both entry-level and experienced federal brief writers to work on mass tort and class action cases in various stages in federal venues. This role is part of the Complex Litigation practice group, based in the Seaport District in lower Washington, DC. The team works on high-profile litigation matters across the country. Candidates with 5+ years of experience may request office placement in other states.


Responsibilities

  • Research and write federal briefs for mass tort and class action litigation
  • Conduct comprehensive legal research using Westlaw and other resources to find applicable case law
  • Work on news-making, high-profile litigation matters


Requirements

  • Exceptional research skills and ability to locate applicable case law
  • Entry-level candidates must have moot court, externship, or fellowship experience and take state bar before start date
  • Experienced candidates must have prior federal litigation experience or prior experience as an appellate or federal law clerk, and must be licensed and in good standing with state bar
  • Must submit at least two legal writing samples demonstrating robust research abilities
row.keywords: legal writing, federal litigation, brief writing
row.skills: legal_research, federal_brief_writing, westlaw, appellate_writing
row.formattedAddress: Washington, District of Columbia, United States

row.title: Visual QA
row.description:
About Company

Monks is the global, digital-first, data-driven operating brand of S4 Capital plc. With a legacy of innovation and specialized expertise, Monks combines global Marketing and Technology Services to redefine how brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its flagship AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine. Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave: Global Marketing Services and ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies.


About the Role

As Visual Quality Assurance, you will play a crucial part in upholding visual standards, ensuring every design deliverable meets brand guidelines and technical excellence before release. Your keen eye for detail and collaborative spirit ensure work is pixel-perfect and functional across every channel and device.


Responsibilities

  • Review and QA designs, animations, and edits for pixel perfect execution, consistency, copy accuracy, and adherence to strict branding and platform specifications
  • Provide detailed, constructive feedback to creative teams, acting as the final checkpoint before public release
  • Test user interactions, verifying pixel-perfect execution and seamless functionality across all devices
  • Use Jira, Confluence and other project management tools to document, track, and report bugs or visual inconsistencies, and ensure timely resolution
  • Continuously refine and integrate QA processes within the wider production workflow, identifying opportunities to boost efficiency and quality
  • Maintain accurate, insightful QA documentation including spreadsheets and visual reports to track trends and guide improvements
  • Collaborate with producers, designers, and developers to ensure the highest creative standards are met in a fast-paced environment


Requirements

  • 2-3 years of hands-on Visual QA experience, ideally within a creative, marketing, or advertising environment
  • Strong eye for design, composition, pace and detail, with working proficiency in Photoshop or comparable design tools
  • Understanding of video and sound specifications, with basic working proficiency in Premiere or comparable video editing software
  • Good understanding of digital media specs and cross-device requirements
  • Excellent organization and prioritization skills, able to manage multiple projects while maintaining high standards of accuracy and quality
  • Clear communicator, adept at providing thorough and actionable feedback to creative and production teams
  • Conversant with QA and production tracking tools, particularly Jira and Excel
  • Positive, solutions-oriented mindset with a natural drive for continuous improvement
  • Previous experience in a marketing or advertising environment
  • Experience checking mechanical files from print, in-store and OOH assets
row.keywords: design, visual, qa, animation, video, creative, branding

row.title: Senior UX Designer
row.description:
About Company

Morgan & Morgan is a leading personal injury law firm dedicated to protecting consumer rights. With over 6,000 employees across all 50 states, the firm serves millions of Americans as their last line of defense against insurance companies, large corporations, and defective goods.


About the Role

On the product team, user-centered design is at the forefront of how we create intuitive and innovative products that our users love and depend on each day. You will work closely with fellow UX designers, PMs, engineers and stakeholders throughout the product development lifecycle. You will present and defend solutions using research, data and intuition. Your communication skills are as refined as your design skills. You are ready to take the next big step in your UX career.


Responsibilities

  • Design crystal-clear, high-fidelity mockups and prototypes across platforms
  • Create impactful research artifacts to support or challenge ideas
  • Collaborate with stakeholders and team members at all levels
  • Own the entirety of your work, from sketch to prototype to handoff
  • Self-start projects rooted in research or design thinking
  • Support and build out processes with design leadership


Requirements

  • 6+ years of user-experience design required
  • A high-quality portfolio containing real-world projects
  • Excellent skills in Figma
  • Ability to present work with confidence
  • Comfortable in Jira, Confluence, and Notion
  • Knowledge of Salesforce and Pendo a plus
  • Bachelor's degree in Design, UX, HCI, Communications, or related area
  • Curiosity for design systems
  • Must be based in NYC

row.title: Senior Video Editor
row.description:
About Company

Morgan & Morgan is a leading personal injury law firm dedicated to protecting consumer rights. With over 6,000 employees across all 50 states, the firm serves millions of Americans against insurance companies, large corporations, and defective goods.


About the Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Video Editor to join our creative team in Brooklyn. You will be responsible for creating compelling and visually stunning video content that aligns with our brand identity, working in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines.


Responsibilities

  • Edit and assemble raw footage into polished video content, ensuring high-quality output that meets project requirements and brand standards
  • Collaborate closely with the creative team to understand project objectives, creative direction, and stakeholder expectations
  • Work efficiently to meet tight deadlines and prioritize tasks effectively, even under pressure
  • Incorporate feedback and revisions into edits, demonstrating flexibility and attention to detail
  • Stay updated on industry trends, techniques, and best practices in video editing and motion graphics
  • Maintain organized project files and adhere to file management protocols to ensure efficient workflow and collaboration
  • Contribute to brainstorming sessions and creative discussions, offering innovative ideas and solutions

  • Foster a positive and collaborative work environment, supporting team members and contributing to a culture of creativity and excellence


Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Film, Media Production, Communications, or related field (preferred)
  • Proven experience as a Video Editor, with a strong portfolio showcasing editing skills and creative projects
  • Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, including Premiere Pro, After Effects, and Photoshop
  • Excellent time management and organizational skills, with the ability to multitask and prioritize effectively in a deadline-driven environment
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with team members, clients, and stakeholders
  • Creative mindset with a keen eye for visual storytelling, composition, and design
  • Ability to work well under pressure and adapt to changing priorities and project requirements
  • Dependable and punctual, committed to coming into the office four days a week
  • Knowledge of video production workflows, formats, and codecs is a plus

row.title: Graphic Designer
row.description:
About Company

Myriad360 is a professional services firm committed to challenging and enabling employees to achieve great things. The company is consistently listed among Inc & Crain's "Best Places to Work" and prides itself on an accessible, engaged leadership team and a culture of inclusion.


About the Role

This is a part-time role requiring approximately 20 hours per week. As a Graphic Designer, you will play a key role in developing and executing visual assets that support marketing, sales, events, internal communications, and brand initiatives. Working closely with the Creative Director and cross-functional teams, you will help bring ideas to life through thoughtful, strategic design that enhances the Myriad360 brand. You will create and maintain a wide variety of digital and print materials while ensuring consistency across all visual communications.


Responsibilities

  • Execute high-quality design work across digital and print channels based on established creative direction
  • Produce a wide range of assets including presentations, marketing collateral, social media graphics, advertisements, and event materials
  • Ensure all deliverables are visually consistent, on-brand, and production-ready
  • Prepare final files for print production and digital distribution
  • Apply and uphold brand guidelines across all internal and external communications
  • Support the continued evolution and refinement of the company's visual identity
  • Maintain consistency across typography, color palettes, imagery, layouts, and overall design systems
  • Update and maintain branded templates, design libraries, and shared creative assets
  • Partner closely with marketing, content, sales, and cross-functional teams to develop creative solutions
  • Take direction from the Creative Director and iterate quickly based on feedback
  • Manage multiple design projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines and project requirements
  • Communicate proactively regarding timelines, priorities, project status, and resource needs
  • Contribute creative ideas and design solutions within established brand frameworks
  • Stay current with design trends, technologies, and industry best practices


Requirements

  • 2-5 years of experience in graphic design or a related creative role
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating excellence in layout, typography, branding, and visual design
  • Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, including Illustrator, InDesign, and Photoshop
  • Experience designing for both digital and print environments
  • Strong understanding of brand systems and visual consistency
  • Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills
  • Experience working in a B2B, technology, or professional services environment (preferred)
  • Experience designing presentations and executive-level PowerPoint decks (preferred)
  • Figma or similar design and prototyping tools (preferred)
  • Digital and print production workflows (preferred)
  • Social media and digital marketing asset creation (preferred)
  • Basic motion graphics or animation skills (preferred)
  • Website design and visual content creation (preferred)
